Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When written to fixed-point data, Stateflow treats output of custom code function as a stored integer
Description: If you directly assign the value returned by a custom code function to a Stateflow Data object of fixed-point type, Stateflow treats that value as a stored integer, rather than a real world value. In both simulation and generated code, the value is cast as an unsigned integer of appropriate size and then written to the Data object.
Workaround: First assign the value returned by the custom code function to a local Stateflow Data object of type unsigned int of appropriate size. Then assign this local data to the Stateflow Data object of fixed-point type.?

Summary: Using importdata?or double clicking any data file crashes MATLAB??on macOS
Description: On macOS Mojave 10.14.4, using importdata  or double clicking a data file (for example, .doc, .txt, audio, video, image) in the current folder browser crashes MATLAB?.  The stack trace from the crash includes lines similar to these: 0x000000016df07403 bin/maci64/reader/libmwquicktimekitplugin.dylib+00005123 _ZN11qtkitplugin22GetSupportedFormatsFcnclEv+00000067  0x000000016df07e97 bin/maci64/reader/libmwquicktimekitplugin.dylib+00007831 _ZN11qtkitplugin9QTInvoker15runOnMainThreadEPv+00000023
Workaround: Upgrade MATLAB? to the latest release.?If you need to use older releases, then follow these steps:?Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB??installation.Locate your MATLAB??installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB??command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. The folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.appExit MATLAB?.Go to??<MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/multimedia/bin/maci64/reader.Rename libmwquicktimekitplugin.dylib. to libmwquicktimekitplugin.dylib.oldLaunch MATLAB?.??Note that after you implement this workaround MATLAB? will not be able to import certain types of video files, for example: Sorenson and Cinepak.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect execution order when Merge block is in a function-call feedback loop
Description: When all of following conditions are met, relative execution order of source and destination blocks of a Merge block might be incorrect: The Merge block is inside a Function-Call Subsystem block.? The output signal for the Merge block is branched. One destination is directly connected to an Outport block that feeds back to the input of the same function-call subsystem. The other destination connects to a non-virtual block inside this function-call subsystem. The Merge block has non-virtual source blocks inside this function-call subsystem.? When the Configuration Parameter Diagnostics  > Data Validity  > Underspecified initialization detection  is set to either:  Classic .  Simplified  and the Outport block directly connected to the Merge block has its Source of initial output value  parameter set to Input signal , or Source of initial output value  is set to Dialog  and Initial output  is set to [] .   In this case, the destination non-virtual block of the Merge block might be executed before the source block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Merge block output connected to an Outport block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Functions imported from Simulink into a Stateflow Chart may not be called if the Chart contains a comment with certain Unicode characters
Description: Simulink Functions called from Stateflow might not be called properly when all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses C Action Language The function?is defined in a Simulink Function block at the Simulink model level.  The State actions or Transition labels which call the function also have code comments containing at least one Unicode character that does not have an ASCII character equivalent. The function call?is?after the Unicode comment.??  Any side-effects of the function call will not happen and the function will always return 0. No warning or notification is given to the user. This affects both simulation and code generation.
Workaround: You can use either of the following to work around this issue:Remove comments containing Unicode characters from State actions and Transition labels.Move the Simulink Function Block inside the Stateflow Chart.

Summary: Error during parallel build of model hierarchy
Description: MATLAB throws an error during simulation or code generation when all of the following conditions are true: Configuration parameter Enable parallel model reference rebuilds  is selected for the top model. The model hierarchy has at least three levels, including the top model. Simulation or code generation builds simulation targets for all referenced models in the hierarchy. The slprj folder and MEX files are removed after the build. The original simulation cache folder is moved, deleted, or renamed. The new simulation cache folder is missing a Simulink cache file for a referenced model and contains the Simulink cache file for its parent model. The Simulink cache file of the parent model was created by a parallel build.  The subsequent build aborts with this error:     Error using RTW/transformPaths>locFindmissingPathAndthrowException    The path 'C:\myWorkingFolder\slprj\sim\MidModel' does not exist. - Show complete stack trace        Error using RTW/transformPaths        The path does not exist - Show complete stack trace?    Error while bringing model references of model TopModel up to date:  see errors reported above for details.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Compiling generated code can throw a macro re-definition warning
Description: Embedded Coder throws a "macro re-defined"  warning when compiling the generated code of a model with all these properties: CustomSymbolStrMacro  set to a non-default value with length equal to MaxIdLength - 7  InlinedPrmAccess  set to Macro   The model may also contain blocks like Data Store Memory and Discrete Integrator.
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 4, R2018b Update 1, R2019a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect representation of down-counting array subscript with constant unsigned start and stop value
Description: The simulation and code generation behavior of MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow might incorrectly represent a colon expression used to subscript an array when?the colon expression?satisfies all of the following conditions: The starting, increment, and ending values are compile-time constants The starting and ending values have an unsigned integer type The increment value is negative  For example, consider model_example_1868474.slx  in the attached file issue_example_1868474.zip . This model contains a MATLAB Function block with the content: function issue_1868474()A=[17    24     1     8    15;   23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(uint32(10):-1:uint32(2));showTemp(temp);end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);end Executing?this code in MATLAB produces the correct output:? 18 ? ?12 ? ? 6 ? ? 5 ? ?24 ? ?11 ? ?10 ? ? 4 ? ?23  Executing the Simulink model , you get an incorrect output:  18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18  Execution of code generated from the Simulink model also produces an incorrect output. See also Bug Report ID 1452753 .
Workaround: Use a signed integer type for the starting and ending values of the colon expression.function issue_1868474()A=[17    24     1     8    15;       23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(int32(10):-1:int32(2));end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);endThis can be seen in model_workaround_1868474.slx in the attached file issue_example_1868474.zip.

Summary: Type mismatch error when assigning an expression to a local data that has the same type
Description: When using MATLAB action language, Stateflow might throw a type mismatch error when you have an assignment to a local data object that resolves to a Simulink Signal, even if the type of the expression being assigned is the same as the type of the local data.
Workaround: Use an explicit cast in the assignment:Local_A = cast(expr, 'like', Local_A)?

Summary: Unexpected license error can occur if local workers in a Parallel Computing Toolbox pool are idle for longer than FlexLM license timeout period
Description: When using a parallel pool, a worker can be idle, that is, notexecuting any MATLAB code. For example, a worker can be idle when the client is not executing anyparallel language constructs such as parfor, parfeval, or spmd, or when not allworkers are busy executing parallel constructs, such as the last few iterationsof a parfor loop.  If a worker is idle for longer than the FlexLM license timeoutperiod, then the license is returned. When the worker resumes executingMATLAB code, it does not correctly reacquire the license. This condition leads to thefollowing license error: ? License checkout failed.??License Manager Error -5??Cannot find a license for Distrib_Computing_Toolbox.????Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: ??https:/ / www.mathworks.com/ support/ lme/ R2018a/ 5 ????Diagnostic Information:??Feature: Distrib_Computing_Toolbox ??License path: 27001@ah-pmasoero ??Licensing error: -5, 0.??License checkout failed.??License Manager Error -5??Cannot find a license for Distrib_Computing_Toolbox.????Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: ??https:/ / www.mathworks.com/ support/ lme/ R2018a/ 5 ????Diagnostic Information:??Feature: Distrib_Computing_Toolbox ??License path: 27001@ah-pmasoero ??Licensing error: -5, 0.??Error using distcompserialize??Unable to checkout a license for the Parallel Computing Toolbox.?
Fixed In: R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect results are possible for cascading Unit Delay blocks inside Switch Case Action Subsystem block
Description: The state for a Unit Delay block may incorrectly be a local variable in the generated code for a model that meets all of these conditions:  The Unit Delay blocks are inside a Switch Case Action Subsystem block. The output of one Unit Delay block feeds into another Unit Delay block. The Unit Delay block outputs are vectors or a matrices. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Enable local block outputs  parameter is selected.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1796701.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script in each MATLAB session before opening your model.?
Fixed In: R2016b Update 7, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 5, R2018b, R2019a

Summary: MATLAB crashes if an attempt is made to grow a read-only property by assigning to an out-of-bounds index,when the initial property value is an empty array.
Description: Attempting to set the value of a read-only property should throw the error message: "You cannot set the read-only property ... ". However, MATLAB crashes if you attempt to grow the property value by assign to an out-of-bounds index when the property value is empty (that is, the value of the property has at least one dimension equal to zero). Example:? Consider the two classes Property and PropertyList as shownbelow:? classdef Property  properties  ? ? propName;  end  methods  ? ? function obj = Property(name)  ? ? ? ? obj.propName = name;  ? ? end  end  end  classdef PropertyList  ? ? properties(SetAccess = private)   ? ? ? ? propList % read-only property   ? ? end   ? ? methods  ? ? ? ? function obj = PropertyList  ? ? ? ? ? ? obj.propList = Property.empty; %Initializes property value to empty?   ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  end?  If the user attempts to set the value of an out-of-bounds element of the read-only property 'propList', then MATLAB will crash.? >> p = PropertyList;  >> p.propList(1) = Property('first') %MATLAB crashes

Summary: Unused local variables in model initialization function
Description: Simulink Coder can generate unused local variables in the model_initialize  function under these following conditions: The model contains a user written S-function that generates C/C++ code utilizing a TLC implementation. The user written S-function implements TLC based expression folding. The user written S-function TLC implementation has a block initialization function.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2018a Update 4, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is possible for scalar Data Store Memory block value that is read inside reusable subsystem
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: A Data Store Memory block?is read inside a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem has a single output. The Data Store Memory block is not written in the parent model of the subsystem. The Data Store Memory block variable is a scalar.  In the incorrect?code, the Data Store Memory block value may be incorrectly defined as a local variable which is not initialized.?
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file,?workaroundForBugReport1755403.p.Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command:>> workaroundForBugReport1755403
Fixed In: R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 7, R2018a Update 2, R2018b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for??inpolygon may return wrong answers for nonvector inputs
Description: The code generated for the??inpolygon  function allows the first two inputs to have any shape, but it may return incorrect results when the inputs are not vectors. This issue can also occur in a MATLAB Function block or Stateflow chart.?
Workaround: Convert data inputs to vectors and reshape the outputs of the function.? ? [in1,on1] = inpolygon(x(:),y(:),xv,yv); ? ? in = reshape(in1,size(x));?? ? on = reshape(on1,size(x));?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for AUTOSAR target when an S-function sets?ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW?to?TRUE
Description: Incorrect code may be generated for a model when all these conditions are met:? The system target file is set to AUTOSAR.tlc, AND The system has an S-function with the ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW(SimStruct *S, int idx, bool val)  function having the bool  argument val set to 1 (TRUE)? for at least one Outport block, AND The S-function must be connected to a root input port.  In the generated code, the input variable for the S-Function may be incorrectly reused.
Workaround: There are three workarounds, each of which will have impact on code efficiency (increasing stack size):Disable the configuration parameter Reuse local block outputs.?Set the bool?argument of ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW to 0 (FALSE) for all the identified S-function blocks.??Insert a Signal Conversion block at the input of all affected S-Function blocks that set the bool argument of ssSetOutputPortOutputExprInRTW to 1 (TRUE). Enable the parameter?Excludethis block from ˇ®Block reductionˇŻ optimization on the Block Parameters?dialog box?for each insertedSignal Conversion block. Regenerate and inspect the code to confirm that theproblem is averted.?
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016b Update 6, R2017a Update 3, R2017b Update 6, R2018a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated S-Function from a Simulink subsystem or model might crash MATLAB or return incorrect results
Description: An S-Function block generated from a subsystem or model might crash MATLAB or return incorrect results when it is used in a host model for simulation.
Workaround: Turn off all code generation related optimizations while generating an S-Function from a Simulink subsystem or model.?

Summary: In generated code, order of assignments to Stateflow outputs might change without any model changes
Description: The order of assignments to Stateflow chart output variables in the generated code might change from one build to the next, even if there are no changes made in the chart itself.
Workaround: See?Bug Report ID 1580490?for a workaround for this and another issue in R2017a.

Summary: Code generation assertion error "lcaEntryEdge != nullptr" for AUTOSAR model
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model fails with an assertion error if all  of the following conditions exist: The model contains root inports for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to ImplicitReceive  or ExplicitReceive . The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to ImplicitSend  or ExplicitSend . The model contains a state chart and a loop, such as a while  or for  loop. The state chart uses values of different inports in a way that results in multiple distinct exit paths from the loop. The state chart writes the value of the root outport within the loop.  Under these conditions, code generation fails with an error message reporting the code construction assertion error "lcaEntryEdge != nullptr ".
Workaround: To work around this bug, open the model and modify the loop logic so that the loop has only one exit path.

The block operates correctly when the?Port Configuration ?is set to?Torque .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem with State Reader or State Writer block generates wrong reusable function code
Description: If a Subsystem block contains a?State Reader or State?Writer block and the block parameter Function packaging  on the Code Generation tab is not set  t o  inline ,  Simulink generates wrong code. Reusable functions for subsystems containing State Reader or State?Writer blocks are not supported.?However, because there are no diagnostics for this problem, Simulink silently generates wrong code. The problem only happens with Subsystem that containing the State Reader Writer block but not the corresponding State Owner block. So if a subsystem containing both,  reusable function will work and the right dwork argument will pass in.For the subsystem that only contains State Reader Writer block but not the corresponding owner block,  our fix will disable the wrong reusable functions to be generated. In particular,  if customer specified "Inline" or "Nonreusable function",  we will honor the option; if customer specified "Auto",  we will make it inline; if customer specified "Reusable function",  we will change the generated code to be inline,  and report an error in the code generation report just as what Simulink does for other cases when reusable option cannot be honored.?
Workaround: Analyze the model?hierarchy for subsystems containing State Reader or State Writer blocks. Change the subsystem block parameter?Function packaging from Reusable function or Auto to Inline.

Summary: Custom port ranges in the mdce_def file for the MATLAB job scheduler are not used
Description: The following custom port range parameters in the mdce_def file have no effect when their value is changed: WORKER_PARALLELPOOL_MIN_PORT WORKER_PARALLELPOOL_MAX_PORT  JOBMANAGER_PEERSESSION_MIN_PORT  JOBMANAGER_PEERSESSION_MAX_PORT
Workaround: To change the ports used by the job manager and workers, pass the following flags to the mdce command instead:-jobmanagerpeersessionminport-jobmanagerpeersessionmaxport-workerparallelpoolminport-workerparallelpoolmaxportFor example, to start MDCE with a custom port range of 14384-14848 for starting parallel pools on workers, type the following command into a command prompt (where <MATLABROOT> is the root of your MDCS installation):cd <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/distcomp/bin?mdce start -workerparallalpoolminport 14384 -workerparallelpoolmaxport 14848These flags only need to be included when starting the mdce service on each node of the cluster.There is no workaround for changing these ports via AdminCenter.?

Summary: In a test harness created for a model, signal lines connected to the outputs of the Model block in the test harness do not have 'ShowPropagatedSignal' turned on
Description: If you create a test harness for a model with one or more Outport blocks, the signal lines connected to the outputs of component under test Model block in the test harness do not have ShowPropagatedSignals  turned on.
Workaround: After creating the test harness, open the test harness, select each signal line connected to the outputs of the component under test Model block, use the Property Inspector and select Show propagated signals.Alternatively, you can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.

Description: Test Manager returns following error when generating a report if the test case used a logical (Boolean) parameter override: ?Could not append child which had existing parent
Workaround: R2017a: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1587292?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2017a.R2016b: You can visit?Bug Report ID 1671801?to download a patch that addresses this and other Simulink Test issues in R2016b.

Summary: MATLAB might crash or hang when compiling a model with a common gear block configured in an unusual way
Description: If you use a Common Gear block satisfying both of the following conditions, then MATLAB might crash or hang when you Update Diagram or run the model:  The Common Gear block is rigidly bound. That is, its base and follower ports are rigidly connected through a series of frame lines, rigid transforms, and multi-port solid blocks. In this case, both sides of the gear are connected to the same rigid component. The origin of the follower frame of the Common Gear block lies on z-axis of the base frame of the Common Gear block. This includes as a special case the situation in which the base and follower frame origins are coincident.   Note that both conditions above are somewhat unusual and not how the Common Gear block was designed to be used. In practical models, neither should be true.

Summary: Block icons can?have display issues after navigating subsystems using Explorer Bar or Model Browser
Description: When you change the zoom level of a Simulink model, block icons might appear blank or blurry. This?display issue occurs when you navigate the model hierarchy by?using the Explorer Bar or the Model Browser.
Workaround: Instead of using the Explorer Bar or Model Browser, navigate the model hierarchy using any of?these techniques:Double-click the subsystem block.For masked subsystems, use Look Under Mask.Navigate up using?the?Up to Parent button on the Simulink?toolbar.At the MATLAB command prompt, use the open_system command.

Summary: Fixed-Point Designer license error when generating code from receiver System objects or receiver blocks?
Description: For Xilinx Zynq-Based Radio and USRP Embedded Series Radio hardware support packages, any MATLAB script using a receiver System object or Simulink model using a?receiver block in code generation mode fails with the following error:  "Unable to check out a license for the Fixed-Point Designer."  ? ?
Workaround: Run the required functionality in interpreted mode. In MATLAB, avoid code generation for receiver System objects.In Simulink, use the dropdown menu on the block?mask to select Interpreted execution instead of Code generation. ?

Summary: Names of formal arguments do not match between Simulink Function declaration and definition
Description: The names of the formal arguments of Simulink Functions are specified via a combination of Function Prototype Control and the Configuration Parameter Code Generation > Symbols > Subsystem method arguments . ?The protoype of the function definition (in the .c file) honors the specification of the names, but the prototype of the function declaration (in the header file) does not. For example, ?a Simulink function with graphical prototype out = f(in)  and symbol rule ARG_$I_N$M  will lead to the following function definition prototype: ?void f(real_T ARG_u_in, real_T *ARG_y_out)?  ?but the function declaration prototype will be? ?void f(real_T rtu_in, real_T *rty_out)?  The names of the arguments in the function declaration prototype will always conform to the naming rule rt$I$N$M , regardless of the Function Prototype Control specification or the naming rule specified in the configuration parameter.
Workaround: To ensure that the names of the formal arguments match between a Simulink Function's declaration and definition:Specify the value rt$I$N$M for the naming rule Code Generation > Symbols > Subsystem method arguments.If you have configured the C/C++ function interface for a global Simulink Function block, in the Configure C/C++ Function Interface dialog box, set the C/C++ Identifier Name of each function argument to?rt$I$N$M.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for noncontiguous reads and writes to the same matrix for which there is data dependency
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for multiple reads and writes to the same matrix that meets these conditions: The matrix reads and writes are noncontiguous.? There is data dependency between the matrix reads and writes.? For each dimension, all of the reads and writes access the same number of matrix elements. For each dimension, all of the reads and writes use the same stride between indexed elements.  In the incorrect generated code, a single, nested for  loop is generated?for the multiple reads and writes to the data. In the correct generated code, there should be separate, nested?for  loops to properly represent the data dependency. See the attached Simulink model ebr_example_1536107  for example modeling patterns that match the above conditions. ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, modify the model so that the code generator places the?dependent reads and writes in separate functions.?If the reads and writes are spread across multiple Simulink blocks, you can achieve this by using an atomic subsystem that has the Function packaging parameter set to Reusable function?or Nonreusable function. For a MATLAB Function Block or MATLAB System Block, you?can achieve this by using functions with the specification coder.inline('never'). See the attached model ebr_workaround_1536107 for examples of these workarounds.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code may occur when a reusable custom storage class is specified at the input port and another signal except for the output port of a Model Reference block
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets these conditions:  The model contains a referenced model. Inside the Model Reference block, a reusable custom storage class is specified at the input port and another signal except for the one entering the output port. The signal entering the Model Reference block branches to another signal line. A block on the branched signal line executes after the Model Reference block.  The attached example model shows one such case. In g1534640_top , the referenced model and the Gain block use the Bias block output. Inside g1534640_bot , all the signals except for the one entering the output port contain the Simulink.Signal  object reuse . When the model executes, the Gain block gets the wrong value because the referenced model executes prior to the Gain block and overwrites the value of the reuse signal. The incorrect generated code is as follows:  void g1534640_top_step(void){ˇˇ int32_T i;ˇˇ for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {ˇˇ ˇˇ reuse[i] = rtU.SigIn[i] + 2.0;? ?}? ?g1534640_bot(&rtY.SigOut[0]);? ?for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {ˇˇ ˇˇ rtY.SigOut1[i] = 2.1 * reuse[i];ˇˇ }} ....void g1534640_bot(real_T rty_SigOut[256]){?? int32_T i; ?? for (i = 0; i < 256; i++) {   ???? reuse[i] = 3.0 * reuse[i];   ???? reuse[i] = reuse[i] + 11.0;   ???? rty_SigOut[i] = -reuse[i]; ? }} ˇˇ The code for the reference model g1534640_bot  modifies the reuse variable prior to the Gain block in the top model?using it. As a result, the value of rtY.SigOut1[idx]  is 2.1 * (3 * (rtU.SigIn[idx] + 2) + 11) ?when it should be?2.1 * (rtU.SigIn[idx] - 2).
Workaround: You can implement either of these workarounds:Remove the reusable custom storage class specification from the model reference input or the other signals inside the Model Reference block.Specify the?reusable custom storage class?on one of the model reference output ports.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code is possible for single input/output Logic block with symbolic dimensions
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions:The model configuration parameter Allow symbolic dimension  specification is set to on . The model contains a single-input/single-output Logic block. The signal entering the Logic block has a specified or propagated dimension with a symbolic expression.  When the bug occurs, the symbolic expression is not fully preserved in the generated code for the Logic block.? In the generated code, the expression may be replaced with a numerical value based on the numerical value of the symbols at the time of code generation.

Summary: Compiler error when running a model with custom code?on a Windows platform
Description: ?If all of the following conditions are true:   You have set?Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Target selection > Language? to C++ You have included custom headers or source files in?Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target  In the Chart properties, you have exported chart-level functions  ?MATLAB displays a compiler error similar to the following when you attempt to update diagram or run the model:  void sfcs_request_service(SimStruct *,const char *,int,void *,void *,int,void *,int *)' : cannot convert argument 8 from 'void *' to 'int *'?  ? ? ? ? Conversion from 'void*' to pointer to non-'void' requires an explicit cast  The compiler error occurs whether or not your custom code contains C++ constructs.? ? ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible when the Casting Modes parameter is set to Standards Compliant
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets the following conditions:  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Style  pane, the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards Compliant . In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Style  pane, the Allow right shifts on signed integers  parameter is selected and/or the Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts  parameter is selected. A right shift or left shift occurs on a negative integer. A signed operation occurs on the result of the right shifted or left shifted operation.  In the incorrect generated code, a sign extension does not occur resulting in incorrect results.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can take one of?the following actions:?If you want to keep the Casting Modes parameter set to Standards Compliant, use the recommended MISRA settings by deselecting the Allow right shifts on signed integers parameter and the?Replace multiplications by powers of two with signed bitwise shifts parameter.Change the Casting Modes parameter setting?to Nominal or Explicit.

Summary: Using videoinput to connect to certain?GigE Vision compliant cameras can generate an error
Description: If you use the videoinput  interface to connect to GigE Vision compliant cameras, the following error might occur:  Error using videoinput (line 391)?    gige: Unsuccessful read of the maximum frame dimensions. ?

Description: Edit-time checking can wrongly report states that are reachable from the default path of a parent state as unreachable.This happens when there is a supertransition that enters a parent state and the destination is one of its children.?

Summary: MATLAB System block reports type mismatch error on second run of simulation
Description: When you input a bus or an enumeration type signal into a MATLAB System block, or use a bus or an enumeration parameter, Simulink might throw a type mismatch error when you?simulate the model?second time in the same MATLAB session.
Workaround: Close and reopen the model and delete any generated folders (e.g. the slprj folder) before running the simulation a second time.

Summary: Simulation result may be wrong for Model block
Description: Incorrect simulation result is possible for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a Model block. The Model block contains at least one Function-Call subsystem. At least one output port of the Function-Call subsystem drives the Model block root output port.
Workaround: In?the Configuration Parameters dialog, clear the Signal storage reuse parameter, or at the MATLAB command prompt, type this command:set_param(bdroot, 'OptimizeBlockIOStorage', 'off')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect logic in generated code when atomic subsystem has a single outport fed by a block conditionally writing to its output
Description: If you generate code for a model with an atomic subsystem in which all of the following conditions are true, the generated code might contain incorrect logic involving the subsystem output.In the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Nonreusable function . The Function interface  parameter is set to Allows arguments .  The atomic subsystem has only one outport, and the signal leaving this outport is a scalar. The atomic subsystem outport is either fed by control flow logic in which not all possible execution paths are defined (for example, but not limited to, an If block without an else port, or a Switch Case block without a default port), or by a block that does not write to its output every time step.  Under these conditions a function might be generated for the atomic subsystem in which the output is passed by value, and that output is not initialized nor written to on all execution paths through the function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated for General CRC Generator (HDL Optimized) block
Description: Incorrect code may be generated in the presence of General CRC Generator (HDL Optimized) block when state logging is turned on.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Data Import/Export pane, turn off state logging by verifying Save to workspace or file?parameter is not selected for States and Final states.

Summary: Columns in nested tables, such as state transition tables,?may be missing in Report Explorer reports
Description: ?Columns in nested tables may be missing in Report Explorer reports.? The missing columns can occur when the report is a form-based report or the output is Direct PDF from template , HTML from template , ?or Word from template . For example, t he Report Explorer's?State Transition Table  component inserts?nested tables into a report. In a forms-based report, the tables may be missing some columns of state transition information.
Workaround: Use Acrobat (PDF), Web (HTML) or Word document (RTF) output formats.

Summary: Error after configuring lookup block to refer to replacement Simulink.LookupTable object
Description: When you configure a block to refer to a Simulink.LookupTable  object, Simulink issues an unexpected error under these conditions: Previously, the?block referred to a different Simulink.LookupTable  object. You deleted the old, existing?Simulink.LookupTable  object and replaced it with a new object. The new object has different dimensions or data types. The old object and the new object use the same structure type name.?The structure type name comes from the nested property Name ?(e.g.?myLookupTableObject.StructTypeInfo.Name ).  ? ?

Summary: Standalone applications which dynamically load and unload the C/C++ and FORTRAN MAT-File Library might crash when unloading libraries
Description: Standalone applications which dynamically load and unload the C/C++ and FORTRAN MAT-File Library might crash in the WIN32?FreeLibrary()  function when running on Windows 10, with a message similar to: Exception thrown at 0x00007FFED1256EA1 (boost_log-vc120-mt-1_56.dll) in TestDriver.exe: 0xC0000005: Access violation reading location 0x00000000FE93E718.   Applications which directly link to the MAT-File Library and which do not dynamically load it are not affected by this issue.
Workaround: Visit?Bug Report ID 1506390?to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs that exist in R2016a and R2016b.

Summary: Opening the Symbols?window for a chart containing multiple exported functions can crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes when you open the Symbols window for a Stateflow chart containing more than one exported function. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [1] SLFunctionGraphCatalogMI::findVisibleFunctions (in \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll)?[2] SLFunctionGraphCatalogMI::invoke (in \matlab\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll)
Workaround: Use the Model Explorer to manage Stateflow objects in charts containing multiple exported functions.

Summary: Precision mismatch between simulation and code generation results in the Lookup Table Dynamic, Lookup Table (obsolete), and Lookup Table (2-D) (obsolete) blocks
Description: Simulation and code generation results from the Lookup Table Dynamic,?Lookup Table (obsolete), and Lookup Table (2-D) (obsolete) blocks? may have a precision mismatch for?some input values when: The?Lookup Method ?parameter is set to either Interpolation-Extrapolation  or Interpolation-Use End Values . The datatype of any of input, breakpoints, table, or output is double.  The precision difference is because?simulation and code generation use different formulas to calculate the interpolation output. Formula for simulation:? y = (1-f)*T1 + f*T2   Formula for code generation:  y =?T1 + f*(T2-T1)?   Where:  y  is the output f ? is the?fraction T  is?the?table value selected by the input and the breakpoints   Simulink prefers the code generation formula for blocks with these settings.

Summary: Code generation may fail for nonscalar tunable parameter expression
Description: For a model that contains a block with a tunable parameter expression (for example, "K+L"), code generation may fail if?each of the following conditions is true: The parameters involved in the expression are nonscalar. At least one of the parameters in the expression resolves to a parameter in the base or model workspace. At least one of the parameters in the expression resolves to a parameter in the mask of a reusable subsystem, or to a model argument.  ?
Workaround: Remodel the tunable block parameter expression using Simulink blocks. ?For example, a Gain with the tunable block parameter expression "K+L" can be remodeled as two constant blocks with individual values "K" and "L" that feed into a Sum block, which in turn feeds into a Product block that multiplies the input to the original Gain block by that sum.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect generated code from MATLAB code containing a cell array inside an object where the cell array is used inside a function and subsequently reassigned in another
Description: The code generator may produce incorrect code for MATLAB code with all of these conditions: An object has a cell array as a property. This property is used inside a function. This property is assigned after returning from the previous function.  For example, the following MATLAB program meets these conditions and ?generates incorrect code: function out = foo()    obj = MyClassl;    obj.p = {1,2};    t = usenested;    obj.p = {1,2,3};    out = obj.p;        function t = usenested        t = obj.p;    endend?

Summary: Variable named "feature" in base workspace when running Simulink Design Verifier results in an "Index exceeds matrix dimensions." error or causes a MATLAB crash
Description: Ifthere is a variable called  feature  in the base workspace, runningSimulink Design Verifier causes an "Index exceeds matrix dimensions." error in R2012a and R2012b, and cause a MATLAB crash in R2013a - R2016b.

Summary: On Windows, saving viewmark of deep subsystem with model can?cause error
Description: On Windows, an error can occur if you try to save a viewmark of a subsystem that is deep in the model with the model. This error occurs when the length of the full path name of?the subsystem exceeds the 260 characters?limit for?Windows file copying.
Workaround: To prevent this error, limit the total length of the subsystem full path name to 260 characters.

Summary: Line drawing fails for lines that have more than 25,000 data points
Description: Line drawing might fail?for lines that have?more than 25,000 data points?when the Projection  property of the Axes  is set to 'perspective' . The following warning message displays: Warning: Error updating Line. Attempt to project a Point4d with W==0
Workaround: Do either of the following:Specify less than 25,000 data points.Remove all data points that have the same coordinates as the CameraPosition property of the Axes.

Summary: DOCXPageLayout objects fail to create new page layouts
Description: DOCXPageLayout  objects fail to create new page layouts. For example, this script should create three page layouts but creates only two layouts. import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('output','docx');append(rpt, Heading1('Initial Layout'));initialPageLayout = rpt.CurrentPageLayout.clone();landscapePLO = DOCXPageLayout();landscapePLO.PageSize = PageSize('8.5in', '11in', 'landscape');append(rpt, landscapePLO);append(rpt, Heading1('Landscape Layout'));append(rpt, initialPageLayout);append(rpt, Heading1('Initial Layout'));close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);
Workaround: Use DOCXSection, the pre-R2016a name for DOCXPageLayout, as in this script:import mlreportgen.dom.*rpt = Document('output','docx');append(rpt, Heading1('Initial Layout'));initialPageLayout = rpt.CurrentPageLayout.clone();landscapePLO = DOCXSection();landscapePLO.PageSize = PageSize('8.5in', '11in', 'landscape');append(rpt, landscapePLO);append(rpt, Heading1('Landscape Layout'));append(rpt, initialPageLayout);append(rpt, Heading1('Initial Layout'));close(rpt);rptview(rpt.OutputPath);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Boolean operands in arithmetic operations may produce incorrect generated code when the Casting Modes parameter is set to Standards Compliant
Description: The generated code for binary arithmetic operations may be incorrect if one or both operands are Booleans, the data type of the Boolean is uint8_T , and in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Style  pane, the Casting Modes  parameter is set to Standards?Compliant . The following modeling patterns may produce the incorrect?code:?? A Stateflow chart or MATLAB? Function block that contains a binary arithmetic operation. A?binary math block in which both operands are Booleans.  In the incorrect generated code, a conditional expression surrounds the arithmetic operation. Silent wrong answers may occur because the output of the conditional expression must be 0  or 1 ?instead of an integer with a larger range. ? ?
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by changing your model in one of the following ways:Change the Casting Mode parameter?to Nominal or Explicit In the Data Type Replacement pane, change the Boolean?data type to a?type?other than unit8_T.

Summary: Incorrect AUTOSAR Rte call order for IsUpdated inport that branches to nonreusable subsystem
Description: If an AUTOSAR model contains an inport that is configured for IsUpdated  data access mode, the generated code should call the Rte_IsUpdated  access method before its associated Rte_Read  access method.?If the calls are reversed, the Rte_Read  method clears the?Runtime Environment (RTE) IsUpdated  flag, which yields an invalid output. The Rte_IsUpdated  and Rte_Read  access methods might be reversed in generated code if all of the following conditions exist:   In an AUTOSAR model, two Simulink root inports are mapped to the same AUTOSAR port.?One inport is configured for ExplicitReceive  data access mode and the other for IsUpdated  data access mode. The inports are connected to a modeling pattern in which the inport with ExplicitReceive  access?can be read before the inport with?IsUpdated  access.? Examples include: Use of a Switch block. IsUpdated  inport has a higher Simulink port number than the ExplicitReceive  inport.  One of the following conditions pertaining to nonreusable subsystems exists: The IsUpdated  inport branches. One of its destinations is inside a nonreusable subsystem and another destination is outside the same nonresuable subsystem. In R2016b only, the IsUpdated  signal is inside a nonreusable subsystem and the AUTOSAR schema version configured for the model is lower than 4.0.??
Workaround: To work around this bug, make sure that the IsUpdated inport is read before its associated ExplicitReceive inport.For example, if the destination of the inport with IsUpdated data access is a Switch block, replace the Switch block with a masked block that contains the following:An if-else-action-merge system in which the condition is based on the first inport.Masked block I/O that matches the Switch block I/O.If the above action is not effective, examine port numbering.?For all inport blocks in the path between the IsUpdated/ExplicitReceive root inport pair and their final destination, make sure that IsUpdated inports always have a lower port number than their ExplicitReceive inport counterparts.

Summary: Sine HDL Optimized block outputs incorrect waveform
Description: The Sine HDL Optimized block will not output the expected waveform when the block dialog setting Simulate RAM Delay  is enabled. When this block dialog setting is disabled (which is the default behavior), the block simulates correctly.

Summary: Unable to access multiple PEAK-System devices across MATLAB sessions

Summary: Saving a Simulink model with large subsystems can cause?the system to appear to become unresponsive
Description: If a Simulink model contains any subsystem whose dimension is over certain size, saving the model can take a very long time.?It can appear that the system has become unresponsive. The model will be saved after a few minutes without loss of?data. The size varies depending on system configurations. It's typically around 8000 x 4000 units. Not all models with large subsystems are affected.
Workaround: Turn off the Simulink preference for saving a?thumbnail image. See Simulink Preferences Model File Pane.

Summary: Model build may fail for Variant Subsystem containing blocks with Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters with ImportedDefine storage class
Description: For a model?with a Variant Subsystem containing blocks that have Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters  with an ImportedDefine  storage class,?the model build may fail because of an error pointing to undefined variables in the model_types.h  file. This error may occur because the Variant Control parameter definitions are in a separate header file or have different storage classes than the Simulink.Parameters or mpt.Parameters? specified for the block parameters.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue setting the storage class of variant control variables as ImportedDefine and placing all ImportedDefine variable definitions in the same header file.

Summary: VideoWriter writes incorrect frame-rate for?MP4 and M4A videos on Mac OS X??

Summary: National Instruments NI-XNET devices with XS transceivers are not usable
Description: NI-XNET devices using XS style CAN transceivers are not usable in Vehicle Network Toolbox. Trying to access such a device generates an error.

Summary: Deployed applications containing more than four tables are slow or unresponsive
Description: Deployed applications containing more than four tables are slow to respond to user interactions that involve communication with the server. Applications that have more than five tables might not respond at all.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: TflCOperationEntryGenerator and TflCOperationEntryGenerator_NetSlope CRL entries can produce incorrect replacements in generated code for add, minus, cast, and shift operations
Description: Incorrect replacements for add or minus operations can occur when all of the following conditions are met: You register a code replacement table?(TflTable)  that contains?an entry created with TflCOperationEntryGenerator  or TflCOperationEntryGenerator_NetSlope? and?fixed-point conceptual arguments.  SlopesMustBeTheSame   property for the operation?entry?is set to? false  or is not set.  CheckSlope   property for a fixed-point conceptual argument is set to  false .  You generate code for a Simulink model or MATLAB function that contains fixed-point?types with different slopes.   Incorrect replacements for cast and shift?operations can occur when all of the following conditions are met: You register a code replacement table that contains an entry created with?TflCOperationEntryGenerator  or TflCOperationEntryGenerator_NetSlope ?and fixed-point conceptual arguments.  CheckBias   property for a fixed-point conceptual argument is?set to?false .  You generate code for a Simulink model or MATLAB function that contains fixed-point?types with?non-zero bias.

Summary: Clicking the Find button from a Bus Selector or Bus Assignment block dialog box can crash Simulink
Description: Clicking the Find  button?in a Bus Selector or Bus Assignment block dialog box can cause Simulink to crash. The Find  button?can be helpful for locating the source of a Simulink signal.

Summary: The reset event port labels on a Model block can switch position without the input connections switching, if you modify the names of the Reset Function block within the referenced model
Description: If a Model block exposes more than?one model reset port (In the Model Block Parameters dialog box, the Show model reset ports  check box has to be selected), then the order of the input ports?can change when changing the name of a Reset Function block (but not the Event Name? parameter of the Event Listener block within the Reset Function block).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When replacement function has more than one caller, code replacement?data alignment may not align?data at?requested boundary
Description: The code generator might not align data at the requested boundary for code replacement data alignment when all of these conditions are met: The generated code uses the implementation function of a code replacement library entry with one or more arguments that require data alignment at specific memory boundaries.? The implementation function of the code replacement library entry is called from multiple places in the generated code.??  If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: VHDL code generation for real-input FFT or IFFT fails if input vector size = FFTLength/2
Description: HDL code generation for FFT or IFFT fails with following error message: Error ID: 'hdlcoder:pirudd:assertionFailed'?  Assertion failed:hdlemitters/vhdltypes.cpp:409:type.isFixedPtType() || type.isLogicType()?  when:? 1. Input vector size equals half of the FFT Length.  2. The input data is real only.  3. Summary: Gathering certain tall array calculations can generate incorrect output or errors
Description: For certain calculations, gathering a tall array might generate incorrect output or issue an internal error message.  If you gather both an in-memory array and an out-of-memory array together, gather can return an incorrect value. This occurs when you create both a tall array from an in-memory value and a tall array from a datastore, and you reduce each with an operation such as sum before combining them with gather. In some other cases when you gather tall arrays, you see an error message like the following: Error using tall/gather (line 46)The operation generated an invalid chunk for output parameter 1. The chunk has tall size 0 where the expected tall size is 1. This indicates an internal error.  or: Error using tall/gather (line 46)Internal problem while evaluating tall expression. The problem was:  or:  Error using tall/gather (line 46)An internal consistency error occurred. Details:

Summary: Executing cvsim for model with Configuration Reference returns no code coverage data for SIL mode model reference
Description: Calls to cvsim ?incorrectly return no code coverage data for a model reference with Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) simulation mode contained within a top model?that?uses a Configuration Reference (ConfigSetRef). Consider, for example, a model with an active Configuration Reference where the parameter CovModelRefEnable  is set to 'off'  in the referenced configuration, indicating that coverage is not analyzed for model references. ?Assume that this model contains a model reference with simulation mode set to Software-in-the-Loop (SIL).? In this code, cvsim  is executed using a cvtest  object that is configured to enable coverage analysis for model references while excluding the top model: cvto = cvtest(modelName);  cvto.modelRefSettings.enable = 'on';  cvto.modelRefSettings.excludeTopModel = true;  covdata = cvsim(cvto);   Executing this code should return code coverage data for the SIL mode model reference. However, instead covdata is empty.?

Summary: Output data type of a Lookup Table block with a lookup table object can fail to resolve to the correct data type
Description: The resolved data type of a LookupTable block is incorrect and the?associated?model simulation has incorrect simulation results when all of these conditions exist:  A Simulink.LookupTable ?is specified and? Its Table  or Breakpoints  Value  property is specified with associated data type information (for example, LUT.Table.Value = int8(3))  DataType  property is not set and is 'auto'  (default)  A LookupTable block includes this Simulink.LookupTable  object. The LookupTable block output data type is set to Inherit: Inherit from 'Table data'.
Workaround: In the Simulink.LookupTable, do not specify the data type as part of the?Value property for?the Table or Breakpoints property. Instead, explicitly specify the data type in the DataType property of the Table or Breakpoints.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not honor Alignment property?specified for?Simulink signals (or Simulink.Signal objects) in generated code
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code when you specify the Alignment  property for a Simulink signal (or Simulink.Signal  object) and all of these conditions are true: The value of the Alignment  property is not -1. The storage class? for the signal is?specified as SimulinkGlobal ?or?ExportedGlobal,? and the signal (or Simulink.Signal  object)?meets one of these conditions: The Simulink.Signal  object is used through a data store. The signal?feeds into a Merge block. The signal comes from a Merge block or a MATLAB Function block. Two signal lines share a signal name and these conditions are true: One signal?line?uses a Simulink.Signal  object.  The other signal line uses a built-in signal specification.  The setting for the Alignment? property for the two signal lines is different.?    If the compiled code from your compiler for the target processor would result in different numeric results or crash when using an instruction?that requires specific data alignments but the data is not aligned properly,?the generated code can produce numeric results that differ from simulation results or result in crashes when all of the conditions above are met.

Summary: Deployed MATLAB app freezes when it makes a call to MATLAB's input function
Description: A deployed MATLAB app freezes when it makes a call to MATLAB's input  function. Invoking the input  function forces MATLAB to wait until text is entered. During this process, components generated by the app such as figures and other UI components remain unresponsive to user interaction. This behavior is seen on Mac and Linux platforms, but not Windows.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible if an "always false" signal from Inline Variant block connects to Vector Concatenate block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets the following conditions: Theinput port of a Vector Concatenate block has a variant condition that can never be active (i.e. always "false"). In the Variant Sink or Variant Source Block Parameters dialog box, the Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is selected.?  In the incorrect generated code, the Vector Concatenate block output is not initialized which could cause a mismatch between simulation and code generation.
Workaround: Select?Display?>?Blocks>Variant Conditions Legend. The Variant condition for any of the blocks connecting to a vector concatenate input must evaluate to a computed condition and not "false".

Summary: HTML-only formats not inherited in PDF style sheets
Description: HTML-only formats specified for an element in a PDF style sheet are not inherited by the element's descendants. An HTML-only format is an HTML format, such as?list-style-type ,?that is not supported by the?XML-FO (Formatting Objects) specification . The DOM API supports the following HTML formats that the FO specification does not directly?support: list-style-type?  white-space?   For these formats, however, in the DOM API, descendants of a document object do not inherit the ancestor's formats in PDF output. For example, in DOM-based?HTML reports, specifying this style for an ordered list: ul.rgMyList {list-style-type: circle; color: red;} causes all items in the list to be red and have a circular bullet. In the PDF output, individual list items do not inherit the list-style-type  format and do not have a circular bullet. ?
Workaround: In PDF style sheets, specify rules for every element to which you want to apply HTML-only formats. For example, the following rules define a PDF list style,?rgMyList, that ?supports red items with a circle bullet.ul.rgMyList {list-style-type: circle; color: red;}ul.rgMyList? li {list-style-type: circle;}??

MATLAB displays a warning before the crash similar to the following:? objc[16791]: Class JavaLaunchHelper is implemented in both /Library/Internet Plug-Ins/JavaAppletPlugin.plugin/Contents/Home/bin/java (0x107e6f4c0) and /Applications/MATLAB_R2016b.app/sys/java/jre/maci64/jre/lib/jli/libjli.dylib (0x1298a7480). Workaround: Adjust the default stack size within a shell to a multiple of 4KB to prevent this problem. For example, before starting MATLAB, run:ulimit -s 8192

Summary: 1-D linear interpolation in interp1 and griddedInterpolant returns finite values when the query points are NaN and the first two sample values are equal
Description: 1-D linear interpolation in interp1  and griddedInterpolant  returns finite values when the query points are NaN  and the first two sample values are equal. For example, consider a data set where the first two sample values, v(1)  and v(2) , are equal:   v = [0 0 3 4 5]; % Sample values  x = [1 2 3 4 5]; % Sample points Call interp1  to interpolate these values at two query points xq :   xq = [NaN 3.5];       % Query points  vq = interp1(x,v,xq); % Interpolated values at xq  vq  vq =         0    3.5000 The first entry vq(1)  should be NaN  instead of 0 , because vq(1)  corresponds to a NaN  query point xq(1) .The same issue exists in griddedInterpolant :   G = griddedInterpolant(x,v);  vq = G(xq)  vq =         0    3.5000 This issue occurs only for 1-D 'linear'  interpolation in interp1  and griddedInterpolant  when a query point is NaN  and the first two sample values, v(1)  and v(2) , are equal.
Workaround: To work around this issue, manually set the interpolation result to NaN for the query points which equal NaN.For instance, in the above example, use the following command immediately after calling interp1:  vq(isnan(xq)) = NaN   vq =        NaN    3.5000

Summary: ?Simulating accelerated model reference?models with MATLAB structure parameters and unoriented Simulink Bus vector elements can fail
Description: ?Accelerated model reference fails to simulate when all  of these conditions exist: The reference model uses a MATLAB?structure that contains vector elements (for example, the dimension is [1 3] or [3 1]).? The corresponding Simulink Bus object matches the structure parameter, except that vector elements are specified unoriented (for example, "3ˇ°).?? The bus goes through a Unit Delay block, with MATLAB structure specified as the initialization value, or the MATLAB structure is used in a constant block with the Simulink Bus specified as its output type.  However, these conditions do not cause a failure for normal mode simulation.?
Workaround: Make sure that vector Simulink Bus elements are not specified unoriented and always match the orientation of its associated MATLAB structure value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible?incorrect?generated code?when handle class?defines a default value for a property that is a matrix
Description: If a handle class defines a default value for a property that is a matrix, the generated code might produce wrong answers due to a failure to initialize the property. This failure occurs when: The handle object is passed to a function that assigns only some of the?matrix elements. Between the object construction and the call to the function, the property is not read.?  In the following example, the generated code does not initialize x.prop  to 1:10. Therefore, the result returned by foo  can be wrong. ??? classdef MyClass < handle? ? ? ?? properties? ? ? ? ???? prop = 1:10;? ? ? ?? end ? ? end  ??? function res = foo()???? x = MyClass();? ? %?x is not read between the constructor and the call to bar ???? bar(x);???? res = x.prop(2); ?% x.prop(2) is not set to the initialization value. ? ? end  ? ? function bar(x)? ??? x.prop(1) = 100; ?% foo assigns only the first element of?x.prop ??? end?  ?
Workaround: Do not define property default values. Instead,?explicitly initialize the?property in?the class constructor method.?classdef MyClass < handle? ? properties? ? ? ? prop;? ? end??? methods? ? ? ??function obj = MyClass()?? ? ? ? ? ?obj.prop = 1:10;? ? ? ? end? ? endend

Summary: Model Advisor compile checks fail when run together with checks from the Model Metrics group
Description: If Simulink.fileGenControl  is used to customize?file generation control parameters?CacheFolder? or?CodeGenFolder , Model Advisor compile checks may fail when run together with checks from the?Model Metrics ?group.? Running the Model Advisor checks from the Model Metrics ?group will create a cache folder according to the settings in the Simulink Preferences . Customization using the?Simulink.fileGenControl ?function is not considered, which may lead to conflicting cache folders.
Workaround: Do not use?Simulink.fileGenControl?to customize?file generation control parameters?CacheFolder?or?CodeGenFolder when?running Model Advisor compile checks together with checks from the?Model Metrics?group.?If Simulink.fileGenControl is used, make sure to run checks from the?Model Metrics?group independently and delete the cache folder slprj created by the metric data collection prior to running other Model Advisor compile checks.

Summary: Trying to reuse Stateflow charts with unconnected message outputs might cause build error
Description: When a copy of a Stateflow chart has a message output port that is left unconnected, and its option Block Parameters (Subsystem) > Code Generation > Function packaging  is set to Auto , building the model results in an error. The top of the stack trace contains the following: ### Loading TLC function libraries ### Initial pass through model to cache user defined code ### Caching model source code Error: File: <matlabroot>/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc Line: 2838 Column: 56 The specified index (4) was out of the range 0 - 3 Main program: [00] <matlabroot>/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc:FcnGetAdditionalArgs(2838)? [01] <matlabroot>/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc:LibGenSubsystemFcnCall(2090) ? ?  The same error occur if the message output port is connected to a terminator block.?
Workaround: If reusing the code generated for the first Stateflow chart is not a requirement, selecting any other values for the option Function Packaging should make the build success. Otherwise, the message output ports of the two charts must be connected to some message input ports for the code to be reusable.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement for?certain matrix operations might generate code that produces incorrect results
Description: Code Replacement Library (CRL) entries for?certain matrix operations might generate code that produces results that differ from simulation results. This code generation issue occurs when all of these conditions are true: The Simulink model or MATLAB function contains matrix operations. The Simulink model or MATLAB function uses a CRL that contains entries for one or more of the following matrix operations: Transpose:          "RTW_OP_TRANS"Transpose_Multiply: "RTW_OP_TRMUL"Hermitian:          "RTW_OP_HERMITIAN"Hermitian_Multiply: "RTW_OP_HMMUL"  A code replacement match triggers replacement for at least one of the identified matrix operations. The triggered replacement is on matrix (non Vector) operations.

Summary: Apps produce errors, missing components, or do not open after changing HandleVisibility
Description: Changing the HandleVisibility  property of individual App Designer components to 'off'  or 'callback'  produces errors when you try to parent the component into a different container (such as a panel or tab). This error message displays at the top of the list:Struct contents reference from a non-struct array object.  Error in appdesigner.internal.controller.AppDesignerController/handleChildMoved (line142) ??????????? reparentedComponent.getControllerHandle().handleComponentReparented(newParentComponent,??????????? oldParentComponent);     In addition, after closing and reopening the app,?the components that have the HandleVisibility  property set to 'off'  or 'callback'  are missing.?If you change?the?HandleVisibility ?property of all components in a group to 'off' ?or?'callback',   the app?cannot?reopen after you close it.
Workaround: To?fix?an existing app, perform these steps:Close App Designer.Execute these commands to temporarily change the ShowHiddenHandles property of the graphics root object:?? rootObj = groot;?? temp = get(rootObj,'ShowHiddenHandles');?? set(rootObj,'ShowHiddenHandles','on');Open the app in App Designer.Click Code View, and?use the Properties panel to change the HandleVisibility property?of each component to 'on'.For components that require a nondefault value, set the HandleVisibility property?programmatically in the StartupFcn callback. You must create the StartupFcn callback for the UIFigure component if it does not already have one. For example:???function startupFcn(app)????? app.Panel.HandleVisibility = 'off';???endSave the app.Execute this command to restore the?ShowHiddenHandles property for the graphics root object:?? set(rootObj,'ShowHiddenHandles',temp);To avoid the problem in a new app, leave the HandleVisibility property set to 'on' in the App Designer Properties panel. You can set the HandleVisibility property to 'off' or 'callback' in the StartupFcn callback.

Summary: Generated code might be?uncompilable for referenced models with MATLAB structure parameters
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for referenced models that meet the following conditions:  Child and parent models use the same parameter that is a MATLAB structure. In the child model, the structure elements are assigned to a Simulink.Bus  object with matching structure elements. This is the only reference to the Simulink.Bus  object in the model hierarchy.
Workaround: For the model parameter, use a Simulink.Parameter instead of a MATLAB structure . The Simulink.Parameter should have the MATLAB structure as its value and the Simulink.Bus object as its type.

Summary: Uncompilable generated code for model reference function containing a Simulink.Parameter argument with Volatile custom storage class
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a model reference in which one of its function arguments is Simulink.Parameter  with a Volatile  custom storage class. The generated code may be uncompilable because the parameter definition contains the volatile keyword but the function argument does not contain the volatile keyword. The target code compiler throws an argument type mismatch error.

Summary: Embedded Coder may generate initialization code for root-level inport variables of enum datatype
Description: The code generator might generate initialization code for root-level inport variables of enum  datatype and a default value of 0 even though the optimization parameter Remove root level I/O zero initialization  is set to On . Not removing this initialization code results in an efficiency regression.

Summary: Assignment from a heterogenous array to a non-heterogeneous array can produce incorrect results
Description: In an assignment A(idx) = B?  when the array B  is heterogeneous and the array A  is not, the assignment may not correctly copy the data or mark A as heterogeneous. Subsequent operations on the array A  may not identify the class of the heterogeneous array as expected or be able to properly access the properties and methods of objects in the array. Some subsequent operations could lead to a crash in MATLAB.? The issue is not reproducible in R2015b and R2016a.?
Workaround: Copy an element into the array A whose class differs from that of A. This will correctly mark the array as heterogeneous, and subsequent assignment operations can proceed normally.In R2016b, you can also use one of the attached platform-specific patch files. Summary: Data Storage Memory block with SimulinkGlobal storage class errors in Accelerator mode
Description: If you configure a Data Storage Memory block with SimulinkGlobal  storage class, Simulink will display the following error when you simulate in Accelerator mode: Error while JIT accelerating the model 'myModel'.Caused by:Potential conflicting usages of identifier 'x': a parameter, and a global data store memory 'x'

Summary: Cyclomatic complexity metric analyzes only parts of the model
Description: When?collecting cyclomatic complexity metric information on?Simulink models, the software analyzes only parts of the model in the following cases: Model metrics uses Simulink model coverage to find decision points in the model when analyzing cyclomatic complexity. If the model parameter CovPath  is not empty and a specific subsystem path is set to scope model coverage analysis, cyclomatic complexity is collected only for this subsystem and its descendants. In R2016b, new model parameters were introduced for model coverage analysis.?When the new model parameter CovScope  is not set to EntireSystem , the software might analyze only parts of the model.
Workaround: To analyze the full model, clear the model parameter CovPath.In R2016b, also set the model parameter?CovScope?to?EntireSystem.

Summary: Models with Rate Limiter block and model reference can crash while doing simulation
Description: For Simulink models having model reference, a Rate Limiter block in the referenced model, and different solver types in the top and referenced models, crashes during simulation.
Workaround: The workaround is to use the same non auto solver for the top and the referenced models. If the referenced model does not have any continuous states, it will switch to a discrete solver, so use an explicit solver (ode45 for variable step and ode3 for fixed step) for the top model. This can be seen by doing update diagram (Ctrl-D) on the referenced model, and the solver displayed at the bottom right corner will change to a discrete solver (VariableStepDiscrete or FixedStepDiscrete).

Summary: Incorrect Relational Boundary coverage for saturation block with differing fixed-point input/output datatypes
Description: When the input and output signals of a Saturation block are configured to use different fixed-point datatypes, the Relational Boundary coverage results reported for this block might be incorrect due to an improper datatype conversion by the Model Coverage tool. This bug may cause satisfied Relational Boundary coverage objectives to be reported as unsatisifed and vice-versa. For example, consider a Saturation block with an Upper limit  set to 0.5  and Output data type  set to fixdt(1, 16, 7)  which represents a signed fixed-point datatype with word length of 16 and fraction length of 7. ?The input to the Saturation block comes from a Constant block with a value of 0.5  and Output data type  set to ?fixdt(1, 16, 8) ? representing a signed fixed-point datatype with word length of 16 and fraction length of 8. ?Note the difference in the fraction length used by the input and output datatypes. When simulated, we would expect the Coverage analysis to report that the Saturation block's Relational Boundary objective 'input - upper limit ' would be satisfied for outcome 0  (indicating that the input signal and upper limit are equal). ?However,?the Coverage analysis reports that none of the Relational Boundary objective outcomes for this block are satisfied.
Workaround: When the input signal to a Saturation block uses a fixed-point datatype, ensure that the block's output makes use of the same datatype. ?This can be done by setting the Saturation block's field Output data type to 'Inherit: Same as input'.If it is necessary for the output datatype to differ, consider instead connecting the output of the Saturation block to a Data Type Conversion block rather than explicitly setting the datatype via the Output data type field of the Saturation block.

Summary: Code generation for a division with a constant input may fail
Description: Generating code for a division when the numerator is a constant may result in the error message "Unexpected or internal error encountered".  To observe the code generation error, all the following must be true:? Inline invariant signals ?must be enabled from the?Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters ?pane The optimization?Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions ?must be enabled from?Configuration Parameters > Optimization ?pane The output type of the division operation must be signed The numerator must be a signed positive constant The denominator must be an unsigned signal?
Workaround: There are three known workarounds to avoid this bug:Set the default parameter behavior to?Tunable?in?Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and ParametersDisable the optimization?Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions?in?Configuration Parameters > OptimizationEnsure the numerator and denominator inputs to the division have the same signedness (either both are signed or both are unsigned)

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) can become unresponsive
Description: After extended operation, MATLAB Job Scheduler (MJS) can become unresponsive. Operations such as cluster discovery, parcluster, and nodestatus hang indefinitely. This also causes the MATLAB client to block, requiring it to be restarted.
Workaround: To recover from this problem, restart MJS. You must do this from the command line as Admin Center is also affected by this problem. On the head node run the following commands.Linux/Mac OS:?cd <matlabroot>/toolbox/distcomp/binsudo ./mdce stopsudo ./mdce start?Windows: ?cd <matlabroot>/toolbox/distcomp/binmdce stopmdce start??Here <matlabroot> is the root of your MATLAB Distributed Computing Server installation. Note that it will take some time for the job manager and workers to restart.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a subsystem with a single output and nonreusable function packaging with arguments
Description: For a model that contains a subsystem that meets the following conditions, the code generator might incorrectly pass the subsystem output through the function return value instead of by reference: 1. The subsystem?has a single output. 2. In the Subsystem Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Nonreusable function . The Function interface  parameter is set to Allows arguments . 3. Inside the subsystem, the subsystem output is conditionally written. 4. The subsystem output is a scalar.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?generated code?is possible when Casting modes parameter is set to Standards Compliant and the model contains signed bitwise operands
Description: ?Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets the following conditions:   The model performs a bitwise operation using signed operands in a Stateflow chart, a MATLAB Function block,?or bitwise blocks.  One or more operands is negative.  The model contains signed fixed point data types that are larger than the target signed integer data type. The width of the fixed point data type does not match?the target built-in data type.   ? ?
Workaround: Change the setting of the Casting modes parameter to Nominal or Explicit.?

Summary: App Designer disappears and the?thumbnail preview in the taskbar is blank
Description: App Designer might disappear and show a blank thumbnail preview in the taskbar. This problem occurs?after maximizing and closing the App Designer window, and then restarting App Designer.
Workaround: To fix the problem,?use one of the following workarounds.Workaround 1:?Right-click the?blank thumbnail and?select Maximize from the context menu.Click and drag the title bar on the maximized window to restore its size.Workaround 2:?Download the attached .zip file.Extract the script from the .zip file. Change the current working folder in MATLAB to the folder where you saved the script.Run the restore_appdesigner script from the MATLAB command line.

Summary: MATLAB crashes if a model has multiple nested algebraic loops?and the?Minimize algebraic loop diagnostic set to warning
Description: MATLAB crashes when you update or run a model that contains complex nested algebraic loops and the algebraic loop diagnostic is set to warning .
Workaround: Turn off the diagnostic by setting?Configuration Parameters > Minimize algebraic loop to error or?none.
