Summary: Spurious lines on Scope
Description: Zooming or panning while the simulation is running can cause extra lines to be drawn on the Scope. These lines give the impression that there is a glitch in the visualized signal, but in fact there is none.
Workaround: The spurious lines can be removed by:Pausing or stopping simulationAutoscalingZooming or panning again

Summary: Error when attempting to generate an individual HTML report for a coverage data object corresponding to custom code or shared utilities
Description: When you run a coverage simulation on a model containing custom code or shared utilities, several coverage data objects are generated (one for the model and at least one for each custom code or shared utility file). If you attempt to generate an HTML report for an individual object corresponding to a custom code or shared utility file, MATLAB displays the following error:? Error using cvi.ReportUtils.checkModelLoaded (line 38)Simulink model myModel must be open before generating its coverage report  ?
Workaround: Generate reports using the generated cv.cvdatagroup object, which groups all of the data coverage objects for the model together.Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.        Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Models with Simscape blocks and variant subsystems thatcontain model references might crash
Description: When a Simscape block (or asubsystem containing a Simscape block) is a sibling to a variant subsystem thatcontains a model reference, compiling or running the model might cause acrash.?The typical model topology is similar to this:SubsystemL--Variant Subsystem| L--Model ReferenceL--Simscape block Or SubsystemL--Variant Subsystem| L--Model ReferenceL--Subsystem??L--Simscape block
Workaround: Wrap each ofthe variant choices that are model references in a subsystem. The updated topology looks like this:SubsystemL--Variant Subsystem| L--Subsystem| ??L--Model ReferenceL--Simscape blockOrSubsystemL--Variant Subsystem| L--Subsystem| ??L--Model ReferenceL--Subsystem??L--Simscape block

Summary: During simulation, MATLAB can crash with the following message: ?LLVM ERROR / emlrtPeekStack
Description: Sometimes?during a simulation of a model that includes a MATLAB Function block, MATLAB can crash with the following message: LLVM ERROR: Program used external function 'emlrtPeekStack' which could not be resolved!?  ? ? ?
Workaround: 1) Download the ZIP file disable_emlrtstack.zip.2) Unzip it -- find the disable_emlrtstack.p file.3) After each MATLAB startup, run disable_emlrtstack. Alternatively, add this command to your startup.m file so that ?it always runs when MATLAB starts.With this?workaround, stack traces?might be?absent from certain run-time errors. Therefore, it can be?harder to find these errors in the model.

Summary: Model Advisor exclusion doesn't work as expected on Simulink library.
Description: When the user defines Model Advisor exclusions for a Simulink library, Model Advisor doesn't exclude blocks inside the library.

Summary: Renaming?a subsystem might result in incorrect edit-time checking violation for certain blocks
Description: Renaming any subsystem in a subsystem hierarchy might result in incorrect edit-time checking violations for the following blocks inside the subsystem hierarchy:? From block Goto block Data Store Memory?block? Data Store Read block Data Store Write block State Reader block State Writer block?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB for? a for statement with?a step of?zero
Description: If?your MATLAB code has a for  loop with?a non-constant step that happens?to be?zero at run time, MATLAB executes 0 iterations of the?loop. On the other hand, the generated code?might enter an infinite loop?that executes continuously with no exit. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, ?and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Add an explicit check for?the?zero step?before the loop.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for Rate Transition blocks which display buf with?Enable local block outputs parameter selected
Description: For a model containing a Rate Transition block, the critical section of the generated code that protects a global buffer may be unsafe under the following conditions:?? Code Generation flag condition:? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the?All Parameter? tab, the?Enable local block outputs? parameter?is set to?on .?  ?Rate Transition block conditions:?   The input sample time of the Rate Transition block is?faster than the output sample time. The output sample time?is a multiple of the input sample time. In the Rate Transition block parameters dialog box, the Ensure data integrity during data transfer  parameter is selected. In the Rate Transition block?parameters dialog box, the Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  parameter is not selected. The Rate Transition block displays buf  on the block icon because in?the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Solver  pane, you select Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer  and Whenever Possible  or Never (minimum delay)  for Deterministic data transfer .?buf  signifies that a model contains such a type of Rate Transition block.   In the incorrect generated code, the global variable may be localized outside of the protection code.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the All Parameters tab, set the Enable local block outputs parameter to off. Note that turning this parameter off may cause ROM consumption?to increase.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect generated code for cell array that contains a class with a property that has a custom setter or getter
Description: If you generate code for a cell array that contains a class with a property that has a custom setter or getter, the generated code might be incorrect. For example, consider the following code: vvc2 = cell(1,3);vvc2{1} = MyClass();vvc2{1}.prop = 42;out = vvc2{1}.prop;  If MyClass  is a MATLAB class with a property prop that has a setter or getter  , ?the C code generated for this?code might?compute the wrong value for out .
Workaround: Assign the class to ?a variable. Then, assign the variable to the cell array. For example:t ?= MyClass();t.prop = 42;vvc2{1} = t;???

Summary: Reading a large number of files using ImageDatastore causes memory leaks
Description: ImageDatastore  object leaks memory when reading a large number of files in a loop. For example, create an ImageDatastore with 1000 files: imds = imageDatastore(repmat({'street1.jpg';'peppers.png'}, 500, 1));?  Load data using the imageDatastore in a loop: imds.ReadSize = 100;  for j = 1:100  while hasdata(imds)   [images, info] = read(imds);?    end  reset(imds);?   end?     After running the code, you should see the memory usage has increased by about 100 MB, and?using "clear all " does not reduce it. Adjusting the size of? "j " in the loop?can make the leak more severe.
Workaround: Use R2016b attachment from the cumulative bug report 1532495: https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/1532495

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected simulation behavior of Triggered Subsystem block controlled by a signal with custom data type registered by C S-function
Description: If you register a custom data type in a C S-function, and use a signal with this data type as the control signal input for a Triggered Subsystem block, the subsystem might not execute when expected in simulation. Simulink does not correctly interpret negative values of signals in this situation.

Summary: splitEachLabel method returns an error when number of files is unevenly split among the output datastores
Description: splitEachLabel ?method produces an error when the number of files, in the?imageDatastore ?from a specified location,? cannot be divided evenly into percentages. As a consequence, one of the datastores will not match the correct split percentage. For example, create an ImageDatastore  with 28 files: imds = imageDatastore(repmat({'peppers.png'}, 28,1));  Assign labels to the images: ? imds.Labels = repmat([1:7]',4,1);  Split the datastore into 6 parts:? N = 6;  splits ?= num2cell(repmat(1/N,1,N));  A call to splitEachLabel now produces an indexing error: [subds{1:N}] = splitEachLabel(imds, splits{:});
Workaround: Visit?Bug Report ID?1532495?to download patches that address this and other bugs that exist in R2016a and R2016b.?

Summary: Unused variable compiler warning is possible for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks
Description: The generated code may contain unguarded local variable definitions for a model containing Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks. These unguarded local variable definitions are possible for the following blocks: Model blocks S-functions Stateflow Charts or MATLAB Function blocks that?have Function Packaging  of Reusable function  Blocks for?viewing simulation results such as?Scope?and Display blocks  The warning?may state that a variable is unused or that a variable is set?but unused. The exact wording of the warning depends on the C compiler.
Fixed In: R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a, R2017b

Summary: Cannot compile?generated code?when C API selected for parameters
Description: The code that you generate from a model reference hierarchy might not successfully compile if all of the following conditions are true for?one of the?referenced models: Before release R2015b, you?clear the configuration parameter Optimization > Signals and Parameters > I nline parameters . In R2015b and later releases, you set Default parameter behavior  to Tunable . Before release R2016b, you set the configuration parameter Code Generation > Interface >?Interface  to C API  and select parameters .?In R2016b and later releases, you select Generate C API for parameters . The?code generated from the model?does not?allocate memory for any?tunable block?parameters.
Workaround: Because the referenced model does not?require tunable parameters,?disable the generation of the C API for parameter data:Before R2016b, clear the configuration parameter Code Generation > Interface > parameters.In R2016b and later releases, clear the configuration parameter Generate C API for parameters.

Summary: Tuning parameters during Rapid Accelerator mode simulation might not have an effect
Description: Changing a parameter value during simulation might not have an effect. This issue occurs in the following model configuration: The model is simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode. A block with a tunable parameter has a mask with a mask parameter defined. The block's tunable parameter references the mask parameter.
Workaround: Create a subsystem containing the block with the tunable parameter. Then move the mask from the original block to the subsystem. The block's tunable parameter now references the mask parameter on the subsystem.

Summary: Uncompilable code for a Stateflow chart?inside?of a?Variant subsystem
Description: The code generator may generate uncompilable code for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Stateflow?chart. In the Block Parameters dialog box, on the Code?Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function .? The Stateflow chart is inside of?a Variant subsystem.  In the uncompilable code, the chart's initialization function contains initialization statements for variables that are local to the output function and not the initialization function. The compiler error occurs because the initialization function does not contain the declarations for these variables.? ?
Workaround: For the Stateflow chart, change the function packaging parameter?to Nonreusable function or Inlined.?

Summary: Possible wrong answer?for?simulation and code generation?for Sum block whose Accumulator data type value is fixed-point with?bias
Description: Possible wrong answer for simulation and code generation might occur?under these conditions: Model contains a Sum block The Sum block has Accumulator data type  parameter set to a fixed-point data type?with?bias The Sum block has Output data type ?parameter set to single  or double .
Workaround: Set the??Output data type?parameter of the Sum block to?Inherit: Same as accumulator.Add and connect a?Data Type Conversion block to the output of the Sum block.Set the Output data type parameter of the?Data Type Conversion block to single or double.

Summary: Incorrect results?when latching input signal of a triggered subsystem with different rates
Description: You?can select the Latch input by delaying outside sign  parameter for an Inport block inside a triggered subsystem. Selecting this parameter latches the value of the input signal for the?triggered subsystem at the previous time step. However, when the?source of this input signal and the triggered subsystem have different rates, selecting this parameter produces incorrect results, as shown in attached example 'mG1484198.slx'.
Workaround: If the source of the?input signal has a different rate from the triggered subsystem, consider one of the following:If a latch is not needed,?clear?the Latch input by delaying outside sign parameter check box?on the corresponding Inport block inside the triggered subsystem, as shown in attached example 'mG1484198_Workaround1.slx'.If a latch is necessary, insert a Rate Transition block between the original input signal and the input port of the triggered subsystem, as shown in attached example 'mG1484198_Workaround2.slx'.

Summary: Uninitialized temporary variable generated for AUTOSAR model when subsystem output signal branches to root outport and subsystem inport
Description: ?An AUTOSAR model might generate an uninitialized temporary variable if both of the following conditions exist:  The model contains multiple subsystems with the same rate. The output signal of one subsystem connects to a root outport, and additionally branches to the inport of another subsystem.   In many (but not all) cases, the source subsystem is configured as nonreusable and the root outport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access mode ExplicitSend . Under these conditions, the generated C function code for the second, receiving?subsystem declares and uses an uninitialized temporary local variable.
Workaround: To work around this bug, do either of the following:Configure the subsystem that connects to the root outport to be reusable. For example, open the block parameters dialog box?for the subsystem and set Function packaging to Reusable function.On the signal branch that connects the subsystem to the root outport, insert a Gain block with gain value 1. Set?the storage class of the signal to be SimulinkGlobal. For example, right-click the signal?to the right of?the Gain block and?open the Signal Properties dialog?box. Enter a value for Signal name?and, in the Code Generation tab, set Storage class to?SimulinkGlobal.

Description: When you create a custom storage class that specifies static initialization and apply?it to a signal or state, the?generated code can?initialize the?data to?an incorrect value if either of these conditions are true: You apply the?custom storage class directly to the signal line?or block?state in the model, for example, by using the?Code Generation  tab in the?Signal Properties  dialog box.? Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem can be triggered incorrectly if control signal is of a fixed-point data type.
Description: There are cases where a triggered subsystem can be triggered when it should not be, if its control signal is of a fixed-point data type. For example, if a triggered subsystem is controlled by a constant signal with a negative fixed-point value, the subsystem will be triggered (even though the signal's value never crosses zero).
Workaround: Do not use fixed-point signals as control signals for triggered subsystems.? Instead, use other blocks, such as "Compare to Zero" to evaluate the value of the signal and then use the resulting integer / boolean signal as the control signal for the triggered subsystem.

Summary: Stateflow charts which call exported functions with bus types can sometimes result in a crash
Description: MATLAB can crash when you simulate or generate code for a model with a Stateflow chart that calls a function exported by another chart if all of the following conditions are met:  Some of the inputs or outputs of the function are of Simulink.Bus type. The caller chart passes in a local data (not a chart input or output) to the function. There are no other chart ?inputs or outputs with that data type.  The generated crash error log contains: ?failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\stateflow\cdr\cdr_transform_io_bus.cpp    ?
Workaround: Change the local data being passed to the exported function to a chart output instead. In order to prevent warnings in Simulink, connect this output to a terminator block.

Summary: Calling intlinprog with all-zero constraint coefficients crashes MATLAB.
Description: If you set all-zero coefficients for the intlinprog ?A  or Aeq  arguments, MATLAB crashes.
Workaround: Provide intlinprog with an empty array (i.e. []) instead of a zero array for A or Aeq?.

Summary: Stop time value specified through a variable does not get re-evaluated in Fast restart
Description: If the model parameter?StopTime   is specified as a variable and Fast restart is enabled for a simulation and the value of the variable is modified in between simulations in Fast restart, then the first simulation immediately following the tuning of the value of StopTime  will ignore the new value of StopTime  parameter. For example: % load a model? model = 'vdp';  load_system(model); ? ? ? ? ? ? % create a variable in base workspace? stop_time = 30;?? % set the StopTime parameter to a variable?and enable Fast restart ?? set_param(model, 'FastRestart', 'on', 'Stoptime','stop_time');?  out1 = sim(model); ?%first sim  % tune the stop_time value stop_time = 20;? out2 = sim(model); ? %second sim out3 = sim(model); ? %third sim The first sim (out1)  simulates to stop time 30. However, the second sim  also simulates to time 30, and not to time 20 as would be expected. The third sim  properly re-evaluates the StopTime  and simulates to time 20.?
Workaround: Set the parameter for StopTime as a number, not a variable. For example:set_param(model,'Stoptime','30');?? The number is evaluated correctly for every simulation in Fast restart.

Summary: Using?the shuffle?method on imageDatastore causes loss of??fileSize property
Description: ImageDatastore object loses information about FileSize after performing a shuffle operation. For example, create an ImageDatastore with 1000 files:? originalDS = imageDatastore(repmat({'street1.jpg';'peppers.png'}, 500, 1));  Now, perform a shuffle operation on this image datastore:? outDS = shuffle(originalDS);  Read the first image from the shuffled datastore:? [data1, info1] = readimage(outDS,1);  Find this image in the original datastore:? firstFileIndex = find(strcmp(originalDS.Files, outDS.Files{1}));  Since we created a datastore with repeated files, take the first found index to read an image from the original image datastore: [data2, info2] = readimage(originalDS, firstFileIndex(1));  Notice that comparing the contents of info1  and info2 returns  false :  isequal(info1,info2) ? On?examining the?contents of info1  and info2 ,?you'll?notice that the FileSize  property is not correctly copied to the shuffled image datastore.?
Workaround: Visit?Bug Report ID?1532495?to download patches that address this and other bugs that exist in R2016a and R2016b.

Summary: Incorrect plot display using multiple Video Viewer blocks
Description: Buffer reuse leads to identical or incorrect display output when you use multiple Video Viewer  blocks in a model with the BufferReuse ?property set to on .
Workaround: Set the? BufferReuse property? to "off".

Summary: Incorrect conditions guarding local variables result in compiler warnings
Description: Compiler warnings about unused variables are possible for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a Variant Source block.? The?model contains a Root Inport block or?Constant block that drives a Variant Source block as its only input. The ground value is assigned to a local variable representing the output of the Variant Source block for the inactive variant.?  Preprocessor conditionals surround the local variable assignment.?  The unused variable warning occurs because the preprocessor conditionals that guard the local variable are not consistent with the usage of the local variable.? ?

Summary: Inconsistency between Model Advisor results and edit-time check?violations for a model?with masked subsystems
Description: If a model has a masked subsystem, there may be inconsistencies between the Model Advisor results and edit-time check violations. Model Advisor checks look under masks according to the check implementation for the input parameter, Look under masks : none, graphical, functional, or all , while edit-time checks always look under all masked subsystems.

Summary: Images added to Stateflow charts might be lost when closing the model if the Simulink model hasn't been modified and saved
Description: When image annotations have been added to a Stateflow chart, if the Simulink model has not otherwise been modified, the images might be lost when you close the model. Loading this model at a later time will show a "missing image" icon.
Workaround: When saving a modified Stateflow chart that contains images, ensure that the containing Simulink diagram has also been modified in some way. Examples:Add a block, and then delete it?Add an annotation, and then delete it?Move a block a small amount, and move it back

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Infinite loop generated when decrementing loop index with a uint32
Description: Consider a loop that has uint32 boundaries: function [count, lastValue] = function_for_loop(start, step, finish)? count = uint32(0);? lastValue = uint32(0);? for i = start:step:finish? ? count = count + 1;? ? lastValue = i;? endend?  In MATLAB, the following functions terminate:? ? function_for_loop(uint32(100), -1, uint32(0))? % count = 101, lastValue = 0? function_for_loop(uint32(100), 1, uint32(0))? ?% count = 0, lastValue = 0  However, in the generated code, when the step is negative, the loop never terminates:? ? function_for_loop_mex(uint32(100), -1, uint32(0))? % goes into an infinite loop? function_for_loop_mex(uint32(100), 1, uint32(0))? ?% count = 0, lastValue = 0
Workaround: Rewrite the code to avoid using uint32 for negative step values.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Global storage class with alias on reusable subsystem output signal may?cause incorrect code generation
Description: For a model that meets the following conditions, the code generator might generate incorrect code for the reusable subsystem output function: The model contains at least two instances of the same reusable subsystem. Both reusable subsystem instances has an output signal annotated with the same global storage class The global storage class has a different alias for each reusable subsystem instance.

Summary: Crash when loading timeseries with character vector in DataInfo.Unit field
Description: When using a From File block that loads data in timeseries format, MATLAB crashes if the DataInfo.Unit  field of the timeseries is a character vector. The issue is not present if the DataInfo.Unit  field is a Simulink.SimulationData.Unit  object.
Workaround: To avoid the crash, change the unit from a character vector to a Simulink.SimulationData.Unit.For example, to change the DataInfo.Unit field of the timeseries (named ts) to a Simulink.SimulationData.Unit, typets.DataInfo.Unit = Simulink.SimulationData.Unit(ts.DataInfo.Unit)?

Summary: Cannot open Model block dialog if the referenced model is called "Parameter"
Description: If a model contains a Model Reference block that references a model called "Parameter", and the model called "Parameter" is open, the parameter dialog for the Model Reference block cannot be opened.?Right-clicking the block and selecting "Block Parameters (ModelReference)" results in the model called "Parameter" being brought to the foreground and a warning being shown in the MATLAB command window.  The same behavior occurs if the model is called "PARAMETER" or "PARAMeter," etc.? The warning says:  Cannot find an exact case-sensitive match for the model or library name 'parameter'.? Using the closest case-insensitive match: 'Parameter'.? Case-insensitive matching will be removed in a future release. Specify exact case instead.

Summary: Transfer a large analysis to a Polyspace server using batch option throws error if the temporary folder has a low amount of space available
Description: During a transfer of an analysis using batch option to a Polyspace server, the following error message is given if the space available in the temp folder is low:  Error: Failure while writing temporary remote archive file /  tmp/  tp0bd58344_9e4b_4b96_9b35_a1417d894a0f.zip??????? No space left on device?  ? ? ? ?
Workaround: Override the temporary folder by redefining?one of these?environment variables, specifying a folder on a file system that has more space available:For Microsoft Windows operating systems, redefine the TEMP variable.For MAC OS or Linux operating systems, redefine the TMP variable.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Root Outport block driven by code reuse subsystem containing Initialize, Terminate, or Reset Function may not generate correct code for export-function model
Description: Incorrect code may be generated from a model if all of the following are true:  The model is an export-function model. The model contains a function-call subsystem that is driven by two function-call root Inport blocks. The function-call subsystem contains a code reuse subsystem that drives a root Outport block. The code reuse subsystem contains an Initialize Function, Terminate Function, or Reset Function block.

Summary: Option 'Go To Cause' in contextual menu points to incorrect location
Description: When a definite run-time error occurs in a function body, the error is reflected on the function call, too. Polyspace shows a dotted red underlining and a right-click option 'Go To Cause' on the function call. In certain cases, the option can point to an incorrect location in the code. For instance, in the example Demo_C, you try to understand the root cause of the dotted red underlining on line 128 of single_file_analysis.c: s8 generic_validation(s8 extrapolated_speed, u8 computed_speed){?...  ? return reset_temperature(v3); /* line 128 */}  If you right-click 'Go to Cause' on the function call, the option points to line 46. However, the root cause is the proven run-time out of bound array index error (OBAI) on line 64.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sample time specification for Signal Specification block is ignored on function call line
Description: If Signal Specification block is used on a function call line and sample time is specified, Simulink?ignores this setting.

Summary: Report Explorer image alignment setting not used for Word from Template and Direct PDF outputs
Description: For Word and Direct PDF outputs, t he Report Explorer image components, such as Image, Figure Snapshot, and System Snapshot, do not use the Alignment property that you select in their component property dialog boxes.
Workaround: To center the image, place the image component in a Paragraph component. For either Word or Direct PDF, specify the Style name as?rgTitle. Alternatively, for Direct PDF only, you can set the Paragraph Style name to?rgImage.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library Mfx_MulShRight_* entries can provide incorrect calculation
Description: AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library Mfx_MulShRight_*  entries can provide incorrect calculation for a fixed-point multiplication operation The Mfx_MulShRight_*  entries calculate the net slope adjustment factor incorrectly  as: ?????output_slope_adjustment_factor * input1_slope_adjustment_factor / input2_slope_adjustment_factor   The correct net slope adjustment factor calculation is:  ?????input1_slope_adjustment_factor * input2_slope_adjustment_factor / output_slope_adjustment_factor  When the correct/ true  net slope adjustment factor is NOT equal to 1 but the incorrect  net slope adjustment factor is equal to 1 so that a Mfx_MulShRight_*  replacement occurs, the code replacement in the generated code can produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Visit Bug Report ID 1435708 to download a patch that addresses this and other bugs.

Summary: Code generator produces incorrect code for model that includes System Outputs block and Initialize Function block
Description: The code generator produces incorrect code if a model includes all these blocks: System Outputs block for integrating external code with code generated for a block that uses a SystemOutputs  function. Initialize Function block for producing code that responds to initialize events during execution.  Under these conditions, the code generator incorrectly places a copy of the external code specified with the System Outputs block in the generated initialize entry-point function.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code may occur when virtual blocks reorder the input data to reduced block
Description: Incorrect generated code may occur for a model that meets the following conditions:?   The optimization parameters Block Reduction  and Signal Storage?Reuse  are set to on .? Block reduction  removes a block in an active code path. Blocks?that are on?dead code paths are not susceptible to this bug. The input data to the reduced block is not in the same order as the data source. This could happen for a model that contains a Mux block or a Bus Creator block that outputs a virtual bus. The input to the Mux block or Bus Creator block are elements of a single buffer. These elements could be the?result of a Demux block or a Bus Selector block. The blocks participating in the above code path are part of the same atomic subsystem.  In the incorrect generated code, the buffer of the block driving the reduced block?may be?incorrectly reused?such that?another block overwrites the value before all uses of the original block execute. The?workaround section contains an attached example?model that?demonstrates this bug. ?In the example model, the demux/mux structure reorders elements of the Sum block before they are input to the Gain block.??In the generated code, the Gain block is reduced because it has a value of 1 . ??The Subtract block?requires the results of the Sum and Product?blocks. The Product block reuses the?Sum block?buffer. As a result, the Subtract block incorrectly uses the second element of the result of the?Product block instead of the second element of the result of the Sum block. The code snippet below shows this incorrect code (generated using R2014a without the workaround): ? ?for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] = g1475400_U.In1[i] + g1475400_ConstP.Constant_Value[i]; // Sum Block output   ? ? g1475400_Y.Out3[i] = g1475400_Y.Out1[i] + -4.0;  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] *= g1475400_U.In2[i]; // Overwritten by Product Block output   ? ? g1475400_Y.Out2[i] = g1475400_Y.Out1[i] + -3.0;  ? }    ? tmp = g1475400_Y.Out1[1]; // Ends up getting result of Product Block rather than Sum Block  ? for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  ? ? g1475400_Y.Out1[i] = (tmp - g1475400_Y.Out1[i]) + -2.0;  ? }      Examples of blocks that can be removed by block reduction - these will all be cases where the actual data is unchanged by the block calculation:  Gain blocks with a non-tunable value of 1   Concatenate blocks with a single input Absolute value blocks with an unsigned  input datatype Reshape blocks Shift block with shift amount of 0  Permute block with dimensions specified for no permutation Single input Logical AND / OR block Bias block with bias amount of 0  Switch block with non-tunable constant threshold and control port is constant? Delay block with delay 0
Workaround: To avoid this bug, you can use the attached patch to disable some optimizations?pertaining to?virtual bus selection. ?To do so, insert the p-file in the MATLAB path and each time you start MATLAB, enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:run?workaround_g1475400.pDisabling these?optimizations?may result in more memory usage and data copies for virtual buses, but the code efficiency for demux/mux operations?does not change.Alternatively, you can?set the Block Reduction parameter to off. Changing this parameter?setting will likely result in much more memory usage and data copies than the using the attached patch.
Fixed In: R2015aSP1 Update 3, R2016a Update 7, R2016b Update 6, R2017a

Summary: Generating a Simulink manifest errors when analyzing a model that uses code generation templates
Description: Generating a Simulink manifest for a model that uses code generation templates fails with an "Index exceeds matrix dimensions" error.

Summary: AUTOSAR inter-runnable variables between aperiodic and periodic runnables are not generated in ARXML files for single-tasking models
Description: Exported ARXML for an AUTOSAR model omits an AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable (IRV) if both of the following conditions exist:  In the model, a Rate Transition block is connected between an aperiodic function-call subsystem and a block with a periodic rate. The model is configured for single-tasking execution. That is, the model configuration Solver  option Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  is cleared or command-line parameter EnableMultiTasking  is set to 'off '.   Under these conditions, the ARXML element RUNNABLE-ENTITY generated for the AUTOSAR periodic runnable incorrectly omits the IRV that connects the aperiodic and periodic runnables.
Workaround: To work around this bug, select Configuration Parameters > Solver > Treat each discrete rate as a separate task or set the equivalent command-line parameter EnableMultiTasking to 'on'.

Summary: Subsystem output is incorrectly initialized in certain cases when subsystem connects to a Merge block
Description: The output of a conditionally executed subsystem will be incorrectly initialized when all the following conditions are true: The Outport block of the subsystem has an initial value. The subsystem output corresponding to the above outport connects to multiple blocks outside the subsystem, either through a Bus Selector or a Demux block. At least one of the blocks connected to the output of the subsystem is not a Merge block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignments to input variable of a function might not be accounted for in generated code
Description: If the code for your MATLAB function makes an assignment to one of the input variables, and then copies it to the output variable, the generated code might incorrectly not account for the assignment. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation for a division with a constant input may be incorrect
Description: Generating code for a division when the denominator is a constant may result in incorrect generated code.  To observe incorrect generated code, all the following must be true:  Inline invariant signals  must be enabled from the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane The optimization Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions  must be enabled from the?Configuration Parameters > Optimization  pane The division operation must be set to Saturate on integer overflow  The denominator must be a signed positive constant The numerator and division output type must be unsigned
Workaround: There are three known workarounds to avoid this bug:Set the default parameter behavior to Tunable in Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and ParametersDisable the optimization Remove code that protects against division arithmetic exceptions in Configuration Parameters > OptimizationEnsure the numerator and denominator inputs to the division have the same signedness (either both are signed or both are unsigned)

Summary: Opening a Simulation Data Inspector view file can crash MATLAB
Description: If you open a Simulation Data Inspector view file by either right-clicking the file on disk and selecting Open  or by double-clicking the file itself, MATLAB can crash. ?The crash does not occur if you open the view file from the Data Inspector itself.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, open the view file from the Data Inspector tool:?1. At the MATLAB command line type Simulink.sdi.view??2. In the Visualize tab on the toolstrip, click Saved Views.

Summary: Generating code for scatter and scatter3 plotswith default size but modified color results in incorrect code
Description: For the following scatter3  plot: ?? X = rand(100,1);  Y = rand(100,1);  Z = rand(100,1);  C = 50*rand(100,1);  scatter3(X,Y,Z);  Open up Plot Tools and modify the Color of your data to be "C". Generate Code from the menu. The resulting code includes the following incorrect line:  scatter3(X1,Y1,Z1,C1);   This should be:  scatter3(X1,Y1,Z1,[],C1);

Summary: Aardvark I2C BitRate and PullupResistors cannot be changed before connection
Description: When using a Total Phase Aardvark device with the i2c  interface, setting the BitRate ?or PullupResistors  properties before?the device connection is open has no effect on the hardware configuration, and?the?default property values are used: 100 kHz for BitRate , and 'none' for PullupResistors .? The following example code?results in the?mentioned behavior:? d = i2c('aardvark', 0, '50h'); d.BitRate = 400;fopen(d);  or d = i2c('aardvark', 0, '50h', 'BitRate', 400); fopen(d);
Workaround: Set the BitRate and PullupResistors after opening the device connection with fopen, as in the following example code:d = i2c('aardvark', 0, '50h');fopen(d);d.BitRate = 400;d.PullupResistors = 'both';

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when passing enumeration inputs to?isobject
Description: In MATLAB code, isobject(x)  returns true  if x  is an instance of an enumeration class. In the generated code, isobject(x)  returns false  if x  is an instance of an enumeration class.

Summary: Update diagram fails or causes MATLAB to crash with Variants if the model contains self-modifiable callbacks or Simscape blocks
Description: If you have self-modifiable mask callbacks (for example, replace_block , add_block , delete_line ) or Simscape blocks in your model with a variant block?, then Simulink fails or causes MATLAB to crash during update diagram.
Workaround: Consider guarding the self modifiable mask callback within the SimulationStatus "stopped"ExampleSimulationStatus =get_param(gcs,'SimulationStatus');if strcmp(SimulationStatus,'stopped')delete_line('Model/Blk', 'In1/1', 'Out1/1');end

Description: If you create a Test Harness with Test Sequence block source for a model that is linked to a data dictionary, and the data dictionary contains bus type declarations, the Test Sequence block does not initialize bus outputs.

Summary: Cannot create an?arduino?connection?on macOS 10.12 (Sierra)
Description: When?trying?to create?an?arduino  object?in MATLAB on macOS 10.12 (Sierra), the following error occurs: ? >> a = arduino('/dev/tty.usbmodem1211','Uno'); Cannot program board?Uno (/dev/tty.usbmodem1211). Please make sure the board is supported and the port and board type are correct.
Workaround: The workaround for this issue in?MATLAB R2016b is to update the MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware to version 16.2.1 in Add-On Manager.

Summary: The InstanceCreated event might not fire when constructing built-in class instances
Description: When constructing an instance of a built-in class without storing the output object in a variable, MATLAB does not?trigger the InstanceCreated  event for the class.? For example, calls of the form: >>?inputParser?  do not trigger the event.?However, calls of this form do trigger the InstanceCreated event: >>?p = inputParser;?  ?
Workaround: If possible, call the class constructor with an output argument.? This will enable the?InstanceCreated event:>>?p = inputParser;?

Summary: Compatibility checks throw assertion?for blocks with unsupported Fixed-In-Minor-Step (FiM) sample time
Description: For Simulink Code Inspector compatibility, blocks that are constant or have parameters that can be tuned must not drive two or more?multirate blocks without using explicit Rate Transition blocks. If a model contains blocks with unsupported Fixed-In-Minor-Step (FiM) sample time, the Simulink Code Inspector check against this constraint throws an error with the following message:? Abnormal exit: Assertion failed  ?
Workaround: Modify blocks that use?a Fixed-in-Minor-Step sample time to use a Discrete sample time.

Summary: Logic analyzer shows continuously spinning wheel when attempting to log a commented-out signal
Description: In?a?Simulink?model, if you comment out a?signal that is being logged,?the Logic Analyzer still lists the commented-out?signal.?When the simulation runs, the tool shows a spinning wheel to indicate the plot is logging data.?When the Logic Analyzer?lists a?commented-out signal,?after the simulation completes, the status wheel?continues to spin.
Workaround: Before starting the simulation, in the Logic Analyzer, select?the commented-out signal and delete it.

Summary: Custom code with C++ comment produces MingGW compiler error in accelerated simulations
Description: Consider a model that contains blocks that use custom code, for example, S-Function blocks. In addition, the custom code contains C++ comments. If you run an accelerated simulation and use an MinGW compiler, the C++ comments produce errors. For example: C:\projects\my_wrapper.c:64:1: error: expected expression before '/' token  //?C++ style comment^gmake: *** [my_wrapper.obj] Error -1? The make command returned an error of 2??
Workaround: In the custom code, change the comment style from C++ to C. For example, change? "//" to "/* */".

Summary: Inconsistent dimension specifications for bus element used with From File block cause error
Description: Simulink generates an error when you simulate a model if?all  these conditions hold: There is a bus object in the workspace that defines the dimension of a bus element in the form [n 1] (for example, [3 1]). The model includes a From File block that loads a file that contains a variable corresponding to the bus object in the workspace.?? The variable has a?structure for data corresponding to the bus object in the workspace. The structure includes a timeseries ?object that has a single time point and defines the data?in the format n x 1 (for example, 3 x 1).?  The error message starts with this text: ????? Inconsistentport dimensions for the data in the variable ...    and?lists the variable?and the name of the file that the From File block loads.? ?
Workaround: To avoid this issue when you simulate the model in the future:In the Bus Editor, select the bus object from the workspace.?For the bus element?that has the?inconsistent dimensions specification, change the?dimension from n x1 to n (for example, 3).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible loss of precision when constant folding large?int64/uint64?inputs to binary max and min functions in code generation
Description: During code generation from MATLAB code, constant folding binary calls to the functions?max ?and?min  with int64  or uint64  inputs might result in a loss of precision. The loss of precision is?due to wrong answers from?intermediate conversions to double.?Note that the issue occurs only when the call to max  and min  is constant folded. For example, the code:   function [y,z] = foo()  x = int64(9007199254741017);  y = coder.const(max(x,x));  z = max(x,x); returns y  as 9007199254741016 ?and  z  as?9007199254741017, because y  is computed using constant folding and z  is not. This issue occurs when all of the following conditions are true: int64 ?or?uint64 ?values are passed to?max ?or?min . One or more of these 64-bit integers is not representable in double. Only values larger than flintmax ?can?trigger this issue.  max ?or?min ?is comparing elements of?two arrays such as max(1:3,3:5) . The syntax that computes the minimum or maximum of a single array does not trigger this issue.  Note that this can occur for calls to?max ?or?min ?that appear directly in your code or in code used by your code.

Summary: matlab.io.saveVariablesToScript and Simulink.saveVars?make 3D matrices numerically complex (i)
Description: When you use matlab.io.saveVariablesToScript  or Simulink.saveVars ?to save a?3-D matrix variable,?you can?specify a multidimensional format for the matrix by?using?the MultidimensionalFormat  name-value pair or the -2dslice  option. However, if the?matrix is double and not numerically?complex (i ),?the generated script makes the matrix?complex?by calling the complex  function.? After you run the script, the value of the complex part of each matrix element is zero. The real part of each element is the same as in the original, noncomplex matrix, which means the saved matrix is numerically equivalent to the original matrix.

Summary: If external input to a simulation is specified as a structure with the Values field being empty, MATLAB crashes during simulation
Description: The following example demonstrates the input which exposes this bug: inputs = = struct('time', [0 1], 'signals', struct('values', {}, 'dimensions', {}, 'label', {}));?  (The key is that the values field is set to an empty array.) When this input is supplied to ?a simulation, MATLAB crashes: sim(model,'LoadExternalInput','on','ExternalInput','inputs')?  ?
Workaround: Set the values field to be non-empty.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reciprocal block with?ReciprocalRsqrtBasedNewtonSingleRate architecture generates incorrect HDL code for signed output type
Description: When you use a Reciprocal block, the generated HDL code might be incorrect when the following conditions are true: The HDL Architecture  is set to ReciprocalRsqrtBasedNewtonSingleRate??  The output is a signed fixed-point data type

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Switch statements using an enumeration with nonunique members may produce a wrong answer in generated code
Description: The generated code may produce a wrong answer when a switch?statement?uses an enumeration where multiple members have the same value. In this case, the code generator might compare an enumeration switch expression by enumeration member order?rather than by explicit enumeration member value.?? Consider the following example:? classdef Letters < int8    enumeration        A(1)        B(0)        C(0)    endendfunction result = main(letter)result = coder.const(@foo, letter);function result = foo(arg)switch arg    case Letters.A        result = 0;    case Letters.C        result = 2;    case Letters.B        result = 1;    otherwise        result = 3;end Calling main(Letters.B)  in?MATLAB displays 2. However, calling the MEX function generated by MATLAB Coder, main_mex(Letters.B), displays 3 .  When this problem occurs, the code generator issues a warning: Warning: Redundant SWITCH cases. Only the first will be executed. If this warning is issued when using an enumeration with nonunique enumeration member values, a wrong answer is possible. This issue can occur in MATLAB Coder, a MATLAB Function Block, MATLAB in Stateflow, and a MATLAB System Block.
Workaround: If you do not see the warning for "Redundant SWITCH cases" then you are not affected by this issue. If you see the warning, make sure that each enumeration member of an enumeration?has a?unique value.

Summary: Coverage report incorrectly displays 100% coverage due to rounding
Description: When you generate a coverage report, it might show 100% coverage even when some coverage is missing. This happens when your model has many coverage objectives because the total coverage percentage in the summary is rounded to the nearest integer. For example, if your model has 200 satisfied decision objectives out of 201 total decision objectives, the coverage report displays 100% coverage due to rounding. However, the report displays the correct exact objective count (200/201), and displays the missing coverage objectives in the details section of the report.

Summary: MATLAB can crash when importing a multi-region tetrahedral mesh using geometryFromMesh
Description: Using geometryFromMesh  to create a multi-cell geometry from a multi-region tetrahedral mesh can crash MATLAB. This happens?when the number of geometric cells exceeds the number of geometric faces in each cell. For example, importing three cuboids meshed with tetrahedra?does not cause a failure, but importing seven cuboids?results in a crash.
Workaround: Create the geometry from the boundary of the tetrahedral mesh. For example, compute the boundary of the tetrahedral mesh using the following 3-by-numNodes matrix?nodes and?numNodesPerElement-by-numElements matrix elements:  nodes = nodes';  elements = elements(1:4,:)';  tr = triangulation(elements,nodes);  [elements,nodes] = freeBoundary(tr);Import the boundary nodes and elements into a PDEModel:  pdem = createpde;  geometryFromMesh(pdem,nodes',elements')

Summary: Test harness created for a component fails to compile with the error: "Unrecognized data type name"
Description: If you create a test harness for a component whose input and/or output contain custom fixed point datatype, compiling the test harness can fail with the following error: ?Unrecognized data type name 'TYPE'.?Invalid setting in 'BLOCK' for parameter?'OutDataTypeStr'

Summary: Tuning of under-specified bus-valued external inputs may be ignored in Fast restart
Description: When the external inputs to a model are specified as bus inputs with some of the bus fields missing (i.e. the external input is an under-specified bus) before running a simulation with Fast restart enabled, then run-to-run tuning of the external input silently ignores any bus fields which were added since the first simulation. ?An example of this situation is attached. To see the problem, unzip the attached file and try the following in MATLAB: ?% get started by opening the model and loading the bus object definition  ?open_system('mExample_g1469269');  load('dExample_g1469269.mat');  ?% assign inp to be ts1 - an under-specified bus input. the model is configured to load external input from variable 'inp'  ?inp = ts1  inp =?  ? struct with fields:  ? ? a: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]  ? ? c: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]  % note the the bus object 'Bus' has three fields: 'a', 'b', and 'c', but ts1 specifies only two of them.  % enable Fast restart and simulate the model. Note the output of the scope.?  % now tune the input. the new input will have non-zero values for field b:  inp = ts2  inp =?  ? struct with fields:  ? ? b: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]  ? ? c: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]  % ?simulate again, observe the output on the scope - signal b is ?still zero-valued (this is the bug)
Workaround: ?There are two workarounds.?1) ?Fully specify the external input structure to match the corresponding bus object definition. For the example model on this page, this means that ts1 and ts2 should have all three fields specified. For ts1, field 'b' needs to be added, for example:ts1.b = timeseries(0,0)ts1 =?? struct with fields:? ? a: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]? ? c: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]? ? b: [1ˇÁ1 timeseries]Now, all fields will be picked up for tuning of external inputs in Fast restartOR??2) Run the attached workaround_g1469269.m for every MATLAB session. This will remove the bug with the under-specified bus input tuning but at a cost of some performance degradation for loading of inputs.

Summary: Preemption in Entity Server block might not work correctly if the sorting attribute used for preemption is not?of type?double
Description: If the Entity Server block permits preemption based on an entity attribute, the attribute must be?of type double . Otherwise, the preemption order?can be incorrect.
Workaround: Change the data type of the sorting attribute to?double. If the entity type uses a bus object or structure, perform one of the following:If the entity type uses a bus object,?edit the bus object in the Bus EditorIf the entity type?uses a structure type,?set the initial value of the attribute to?a double value in the Entity Generator block that generates this entity

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a nonvirtual subsystem inside an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a nonvirtual subsystem that is inside an Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block. The incorrect generated code contains incorrect logic involving the output function of the nonvirtual subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for atomic subsystem with no outports and with Minimize algebraic loop occurrences parameter set to on
Description: The sorted order may be incorrect for a model containing an atomic subsystem with no outports and with the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  parameter set to on . For example, the?atomic subsystem may occur before the blocks that drive it even though the atomic subsystem is a direct-feed-through block. The incorrect sorted order may cause the following: 1. If the Enable local block outputs  parameter is set to on , the simulation and code generation results may not match because the generated code may contain a local variable whose value is used but not assigned.  2.??If the Enable local block outputs  parameter is set to off , there may be a hidden wrong answer because both the simulation and code generation results are incorrect.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:??(1)??Use the function turnOffMinAlgLoopFlagForSubsysWithoutOutports in the attached zip file?to automatically set MinAlgLoopOccurrences flag to??off?for subsystems without outports.?(2) ?Manually set the Minimize algebraic loop occurrences parameter to off?by entering the following command:? ? ? ? set_param(block, 'MinAlgLoopOccurrences', 'off');??Please note this parameter's default setting?is off, and this parameter is not shown?on the subsystem block parameters dialog box. ?
Products: MATLAB Coder, Embedded Coder, Simulink Coder

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameter argument order not preserved when setting ParameterArgumentNames on a referenced model
Description: When configuring model parameter arguments using the model parameter ParameterArgumentNames , the order of the parameter arguments is not preserved.  For example: >> set_param('MyModel', 'ParameterArgumentNames','a,c,b')  >> disp(get_param('MyModel','ParameterArgumentNames'))  a,b,c?

Summary: Model Advisor and Upgrade Advisor throw an error for a model in a folder with a long path name
Description: If you open Model Advisor or Upgrade Advisor from a model in a folder?with a?long path name (>156 characters), it displays?this error message: Failed to create Cache.

Summary: DO Qualification Kit?does not fully verify the Simulink Verification and Validation code coverage functionality
Description: The DO Qualification Kit does not include baseline reports for source code files compiled separately?(test cases TC165_1  and TC165_3 ). As such, the DO Qualification Kit cannot fully verify Simulink Verification and Validation code coverage compliance?to tool operational requirement CC_SC_CMPL " SLVNV shall correctly address separately compiled source files".

Summary: Generated error for bus object mismatch between root-level Outport block data type and received signal is not descriptive enough
Description: Simulation and code generation do not generate the expected error when all of these conditions apply:? A root-level Outport block receives a bus defined by a bus object. The Outport block specifies a different bus object as its data type. The structure?of the signal hierarchy of the received bus and specified bus object match, regardless of bus element names and properties.  When all of these conditions apply, Simulink does not generate an error describing this problem. Instead, the root-level Outport block propagates the received bus to downstream blocks. Downstream blocks will throw errors that the received signal does not match the expected signal. If no downstream blocks exist, the compiler will throw an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: S-function with function-call input port used in a branched function-call might generate incorrect answers in Accelerator mode simulation
Description: When an S-function has its function-call input port connected to a Function-Call Split block, the S-function might generate incorrect outputs when the model is simulated in Accelerator mode.

Summary: Baseline test comparisons might fail in Test Manager if the comparison involves Simulink Design Verifier-generated baseline criteria
Description: If you create a baseline test in Test Manger by importing test cases from Simulink Design Verifier into Simulink Test (note that the parameter DVSaveExpectedOutput  should be set to 'on'  during the Simulink Design Verifier test-generation to generate the baseline criteria), the baseline comparison of the test case might fail due to signal alignment mismatches. For example, when the outport port dimension is 2, ?the signal names in the baseline are Out[1][1] and Out[2][1], whereas the signal names produced during simulation of the test case are Out[1] and Out[2]. This scenario occurs only if the test case steps in the Simulink Design Verifier-generated test case is 1, and the port dimension of the outport is greater than 1.? ?

Summary: Loop fusion optimization does not combine multiple loops into single loop

Summary: Incorrect error message when the model is configured for creating a SIL or PIL block for verifying the generated code
Description: If your model is configured to create a SIL or PIL block for verification of generated code and you run a SIL or PIL simulation with coverage enabled then you get an incorrect message like the following: The BuildInfo file "c:\work\<component>_ert_rtw\sil\hostobj\instrumented\buildInfo.mat" doesn't exist for component "<component>".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for S-function with non-scalar signal input can produce incorrect numerical results
Description: The generated code for the S-function block (built with system target file rtwsfcn.tlc ) can produce incorrect numerical results when the following conditions are true: The generated S-function block?has at least 1 input port.  One or more input ports to the S-function block has a non-scalar dimension.  Multiple upstream blocks contribute to the non-scalar signal line that connects to the input port or ports of the S-function block. For example, a Mux block creates the non-scalar signal line.   These conditions can cause the input signal data to the S-function block to reside in non-contiguous memory. Because the S-function requires that the input data is contiguous, the generated code can produce incorrect numerical results.
Workaround: Use either of these workaroundsIf using a previously generated S-function block, insert a Signal Conversion block immediately before each non-scalar input to the S-function. This Signal Conversion block directs the code generator to make a copy of the signal in a contiguous memory region that the S-function reads. Configure the Signal Conversion block as follows:Set Output: to Signal CopyEnable Exclude this block from 'Block Reduction' optimizationIf generating the S-function block (with the S-function system target file rtwsfcn.tlc), before generating code, configure either of these Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters options for the block as follows. Either of these options cause the generated code for the S-function not to?expect a contiguous memory region for non-scalar inputs:Disable Use memcpy for vector assignmentSet the Memcpy threshold value to a large number (for example, intmax: 2147483647)

Summary: Multiple page breaks in PDF reports collapse into single page break
Description: The DOM API does not support multiple page breaks in PDF reports. The PDF report collapses multiple page breaks?into a single page break. For example, the PDF output from this code inserts only a single page break instead of two page breaks. import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport','pdf');open(d); append(d, Paragraph('Paragraph one first page'));append(d, PageBreak());append(d, PageBreak());append(d, Paragraph('Paragraph after 2 page breaks'));  close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);?
Workaround: To include multiple page breaks in a report, insert an empty paragraph between each page break. For example, ?import mlreportgen.dom.*d = Document('myreport','pdf');open(d);append(d, Paragraph('Paragraph one first page'));append(d, PageBreak());% Insert an empty paragraph to include multiple page breaks in PDF reportappend(d, Paragraph(''));append(d, PageBreak());append(d, Paragraph('Paragraph after 2 page breaks'));close(d);rptview(d.OutputPath);

Summary: Semitransparent surface plots created with?fimplicit3?ignore color changes
Description: Changing the color of a semitransparent surface plot created with?fimplicit3 ?has no visual effect. If the FaceAlpha  value of the surface is less than 1, then the surface does not respond to changes i n the FaceColor ?value.

Summary: Unnecessary indexing computation in generated HDL code for System object properties that are row vectors and are mapped to RAMs
Description: System object properties with size 1xn that are mapped to RAM generate HDL indexing logic that is not necessary for indexing a row vector. This computation logic may contain multiplier and adders. For example, a System object property declared and initialized as below may exhibit this problem: classdef mySystemObject < matlab.System  ? properties?  ? ? DataVector?  ? end?  ? methods(Access=protected)?  ? ? function setupImpl(obj, u)?  ? ? ? obj.DataVector = zeros(1, 512, 'like', u);?  ? end?  ... define stepImpl?  end?
Workaround: Using nx1 column vectors instead of row vectors avoids this problem. For example, initialize a property with zeros using? ? function setupImpl(obj, u)?? ? ? obj.DataVector = zeros(512, 1, 'like', u);?? end??

Summary: Global Data Store block that accessed in Public Simulink function that called in Initialize/Reset/Terminate functions and other function may cause Simulink crash.
Description: 1. A global DSM is accessed from?more than 2?functions (Simulink Function, Export FcnCalls, or IRT functions), AND2. One of these fucntions is?public?Simulink function. AND 3. This public Simulink Function is?called?in more than 1 other functions (Simulink function, Export Function Call system, IRT functions).?AND 4. The number of function accesses the global DSM block is?more?than number functions call public Simulink functions.  ?When all of above conditions are true, Simulink may crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect variable assignment to 0.0 or -0.0 in the true and false branches of if statement
Description: The code generator may generate incorrect C code for a Simulink model that contains the following MATLAB code:  if p?  ?? y=0;?  else?  ?? y=-0;?  end  The C code may be optimized such  that y  is set equal to 0.0  or -0.0  regardless of whether the If  statement evaluates to true or false. Note that the above MATLAB code may be explicit in a Simulink model (in a MATLAB function block, for example). The above MATLAB code may also occur during the code generation process and thus be in the model as a combination of blocks that produce the same logic. The attached model contains an example of when this bug occurs in a MATLAB Function block.? ?

Summary: Methods isomorphism and isisomorphic error if one of the input graphs has exactly one connected component, and the other has more.
Description: The methods isomorphism(g1,g2) ?and isisomorphic(g1,g2) , where g1 ?and g2 ?are of class graph ?or digraph , return an unexpected error message when one of the graphs has only one connected component, but the other graph has several connected components. The correct results for such inputs are false ?for isisomorphic(g1,g2) , and an empty double ([] ) for isomorphism(g1,g2) , because no isomorphism exists.

Summary: On macOS?10.12 (Sierra)?MATLAB does not show Chinese, Japanese, and Korean?localized user interfaces and messages, and the default character encoding is incorrect for many locales.
Description: On macOS?10.12 (Sierra) MATLAB?does not show?localized user interfaces and messages for?Chinese, Japanese, and Korean?locales. ?Because of this, the MATLAB?Desktop, other UIs, prompts, warnings, and error messages appear in English. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code missing for root Outport blocks connected to Initialize/Reset/Terminate Function blocks
Description: Code to compute the value for root Outport blocks might be incorrectly eliminated if all of the following conditions are true:? The model contains at least two root Outport blocks that are driven by the same Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block Inside the Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block, the signals for two root Outport blocks are computed by duplicated sets of blocks. In other words, the two signals originate from the same source block and go through two sets of identical blocks before arriving at the two root Outport blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Initialization code seen with Ensure outport is virtual selected on Outport block

Summary: The memory section associated with a subfunction is unpredictable
Description: Simulink Coder generates C subfunctions for the following modeling constructs:?    1)   Intrinsic math functions (e.g. lookup, fixpt)? 2) Noninlined Stateflow graphical functions? 3) Noninlined MATLAB subfunctions? When an Embedded target is chosen, the user can specify memory sections to be associated with the functions in the generated code. ?? These memory sections are used to associate pragmas with the functions.? In many cases, the memory section associated with the subfunctions is unpredictable. ?? Possibilities include:? 1) The Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Memory Sections > Shared Utility  memory section specified at the model level. 2) The Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Memory Sections > Execution  memory section specified at the model level. 3) The Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Memory Sections > Execution  memory section specified for one of the subsystems. The determining factors  are difficult to predict, and are subject to bifurcation. ?? For example, adding a model block to a model can cause the shared utilities to switch from being dumped in the build directory to being dumped in the slprj/    _shareutils folder . ?

Summary: Closing a Simulink test harness can?crash MATLAB
Description: Closing a test harness crashes MATLAB in thisscenario: A subsystem that has a harness contains a Stateflow chart.  The chart is open in its own Simulink Editor tab.  You open the subsystem harness.   When you return to the model (close theharness), the crash occurs.
Workaround: If a chart is open in its own tab, close the tab before you open?the harness on the parent subsystem.

Summary: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for models with root outport blocks that have the Ensure outport is virtual parameter?selected
Description: Simulink Coder might fail to generate code for models with root outport blocks that have the Ensure outport is virtual? parameter selected. An internal error might be reported with the following string in a displayed stack trace.   CGIR assertion 'T::isa((const Type*)aType)' failed in 'export/   include/   cg_ir/   type/   Type.hpp'     The Ensure outport is virtual  parameter is a new feature in R2016b.
Workaround: Clear the? Ensure outport is virtual? checkbox on all Root Outport blocks.

Summary: Interruption when adding Simulink block to model programmatically might crash MATLAB?
Description: When you add a block to a Simulink model programmatically,?MATLAB might crash?if the operation is interrupted. For example, repeatedly hitting Ctrl-C?during the operation?can result in a crash.

Summary: Inaccurate detection of unused signals by?Simulink Design Verifier?analysis causes incorrect harness model and analysis report
Description: SimulinkDesign Verifier can eliminate unused signals (signals that have no effect onthe model's output) from the Signal Builder block of its harness model and fromtest data in its report. However, in some cases the analysis may incorrectly marka signal as unused, which may cause:   The removal of used signals from the harness model.     This causes a mismatch between the objective status reported by analysis     and coverage results obtained from the harness.  The used signal data to be     incorrectly omitted while reporting test cases in the generated report.
Workaround: Use the data file generated during Simulink Design Verifier analysis to build the harness model.?To generate the harness model without eliminating any unused signals from the Signal Builder block, use the sldvmakeharness command with usedSignalsOnly in harnessOpts set to false. By default, this option is set to false, so running this command along with the model and its data file generates the harness model without removing any unused signals. To view the documentation on sldvmakeharness, at the MATLAB command line, type:doc sldvmakeharnessSee the documentation to learn more about data files generated by Simulink Design Verifier.

Summary: HDL code generation error occurs when Simulink model has model references containing masked library blocks
Description: If the Simulink model has model references containing masked library blocks in normal simulation mode, HDL code generation error occurs: Cannot connect to model '<topModelName>'; please try Update Diagram (Ctrl-D).?  Caused by:?  The model '<bottomModelName>' has changed between the time when two instances of this model were compiled. ?This is not allowed for models referenced in Normal Mode.?
Workaround: Change the Simulation Mode of the design from 'Normal' to 'Accelerator'.

Summary: Incorrect plot on Scope when processing input as frames
Description: Scope could plot incorrect data when the input processing property is set to "Columns as channels (frame based)" and it either has a multirate signal at its input ports or data logging is enabled and set to the "Dataset" format. The incorrect plot is usually noticed after autoscaling, zooming, or panning.

Summary: Creating row in timetable by assigning to new row time creates invalid timetable
Description: When you create a new row in a timetable using subscripted assignment to a new row time, the result is an invalid timetable. For example, the following code should add a fourth row to a timetable:? >> tt = timetable([1;2;3],[10;20;30],'RowTimes',datetime(2016,9,1:3))tt = ? ? ? ?Time ? ? ? Var1 ? ?Var2 ? ?___________ ? ?____ ? ?____ ? ?01-Sep-2016 ? ?1 ? ? ? 10 ? ? ?02-Sep-2016 ? ?2 ? ? ? 20 ? ? ?03-Sep-2016 ? ?3 ? ? ? 30 ?>> tt('2016-09-04',:) = table(4,40);  However,  tt  is not a valid timetable. For example:  >> tt(4,:)Index exceeds matrix dimensions.  >> tttt = Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.
Workaround: Create the new row using numeric indexing, and then assign the new row time. For example:>> tt = timetable([1;2;3],[10;20;30],'RowTimes',datetime(2016,9,1:3))tt = ? ? ? ? Time ? ? ? For example:>> tt = timetable([1;2;3],[10;20;30],'RowTimes',datetime(2016,9,1:3));>> tt = [tt; timetable(4,40,'RowTimes',datetime(2016,9,4))];You may also follow the instructions below to install the attached patch. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value for matrix inside an expression that dereferences a pointer in a for loop
Description: Incorrect results are possible for a model design that results in the following generated code:The code contains references of matrix (in the example code, m ) inside a for  loop.  The?matrix m  is both read to?and written to. The matrix m  accesses have identical indexing (in the example code,?both have i ). The code also contains a pointer to an aggregate type variable (in the example code, s ) dereferenced inside a for  loop.  The use of the matrix m  is also embedded inside the index expression of s .   For example, this code correctly shows the matrix assignment. for(i = 0; i < n; ++i){?m[i] += 1;?m[i] = *(s[m[i]]);}  In the incorrect code shown below, the code generator replaces  m[i]  with the scalar variable  m . However, the code generator does not also replace the embedded access of  m[i]  with  m . Thus,  m  contains the incorrect value.  for(i = 0; i < n; ++i){? m = m[i];??m += 1;? m = *(s[m[i]]);??m[i] = m;?}?  Below is an example of the incorrect code where m  is embedded within a pointer to a struct?s :? for(i = 0; i < n; ++i){? m = m[i];??m += 1;? m = s->data[m[i]];??m[i] = m;}?  The issue affects the following code generation workflows: Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation using a Simulink model?

Summary: Simulink?treats an initial value of '' differently from '0' for a global Data Store Memory block?with symbolic dimensions
Description: When a global Data Store Memory (DSM)?block has symbolic dimensions,?Simulink treats an?initial value of '' and '0'?differently as follows: If the dimension of the DSM is 1D and the initial value of the DSM is '', Simulink silently ignores the symbolic dimension. If the dimension of the DSM is 1D and the initial value of the DSM is '0',?simulation produces an error message that refers to the?Data Store Read block. If the dimension of the DSM is 2D and the initial value of the DSM is '',?simulation produces an error?message that refers to the?Data Store Read block. If the dimension of the DSM is 2D and the initial value of the DSM is '0',?simulation and code generation succeed.   ? ? ?
Workaround: For the?global Data?Store Memory block, do not specify a single dimension and do no specify an empty ('')?initial value.?

Summary: Creating row in table by assigning to new row name creates invalid table
Description: When you create a new row in a table using subscripted assignment to a new row name, the result, in some cases, is a table that is invalid.? For example, the following code adds a second row to a table. However, the resulting table's row names are subsequently returned as a row instead of as a column: >> t = table(1,10,'RowNames',{'a'})t = ? ? ? ? ?Var1 ? ?Var2 ? ? ? ? ____ ? ?____ ? ?a ? ?1 ? ? ? 10 ?>> t('b',:) = table(2,20);>> t.Properties.RowNamesans = ?1ˇÁ2 cell array ? ?'a' ? ?'b'?  You might also encounter an error when deleting a row from the invalid table: >> t('b',:) = []Matrix index is out of range for deletion.?
Workaround: Create the new row using numeric indexing, and then assign the new row name. For example:>> t = table(1,10,'RowNames',{'a'});>> t = [t; table(2,20,'RowNames',{'b'})];You may also install the attachment provided for?Bug Report 1458436, which works around this issue as well.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB for a persistent variable in an anonymous function
Description: When an anonymous function is created, the generated code does not?copy a use of persistent variable. For example, consider this function: function y = foo(x)persistent p;p = x;f = @() p;p = p + 1;y = f(); The function?assigns the later value of p  to y  when f  is invoked, rather than the value of p  when f  is assigned.
Workaround: Copy a persistent variable to a local variable and use the local variable in place of the persistent variable in an anonymous function. For example, rewritepersistent p;...f = @() p;...aspersistent p;...x = p;f = @() x;...
