Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect representation of down-counting array subscript with constant unsigned start and stop value
Description: MATLAB Coder might incorrectly represent a colon expression used to subscript an array when?the colon expression?satisfies all of the following conditions: The starting, increment, and ending values are compile-time constants The starting and ending values have an unsigned integer type The increment value is negative  For example, consider the?MATLAB function: function issue_1452753()A=[17    24     1     8    15;   23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(uint32(10):-1:uint32(2));showTemp(temp);end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);end Executing?issue_1452753 ?in MATLAB produces the output:? 18 ? ?12 ? ? 6 ? ? 5 ? ?24 ? ?11 ? ?10 ? ? 4 ? ?23  If you generate a MEX file issue_1452753_mex?by using?MATLAB Coder and run it, you get an incorrect output:   18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18 ? ?18  See also Bug Report ID 1868474 .?
Workaround: Use a signed integer type for the starting and ending values of the colon expression.function issue_1452753()A=[17    24     1     8    15;       23     5     7    14    16;    4     6    13    20    22;   10    12    19    21     3;   11    18    25     2     9]; temp = A(int32(10):-1:int32(2));end function showTemp(temp)coder.inline('never');disp(temp);end

Summary: Syntax highlighting is incorrect when an app created using a previous version of MATLAB is opened App Designer
Description: When you use MATLAB R2016b App Designer to open an app that had been created in MATLAB R2016a, the syntax highlighting of the generated code in incorrect.  ?
Workaround: To display the correct syntax highlighting, use either of the following techniques. Save the MATLAB R2016a app as a MATLAB R2016b app by selecting Save > Save As. This saves the app in the updated format.Edit the app in MATLAB R2016b and save it.

Summary: Utility functions use Execution memory section
Description: Utility functions may have the same memory section as model-level functions and internal data for a model that meets the following conditions:   In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the CodeGeneration > Interface  pane, the Sharedcode placement  parameter is set to Auto .   The code generator places utility functions in the buildfolder.    If?a model meets the above conditions, the Execution  parameter specifies the memory section. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Execution parameter is on  the Code Generation > Interface  pane. Having the same memory section as the model-level functions and internal data could result in the unintentional export of utility functions.         ?
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the CodeGeneration > Interface pane, set the Shared code placement parameter to Sharedlocation, so that the code generator uses the Shared utility memory section.The Shared utility parameter is on the Code Generation > Memory section pane.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for State Reader/Writer block accessing Unit Delay block state
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of the following conditions are met: A State Reader/Writer block access a Unit Delay block state. The Unit Delay block and the State Reader/Writer block are in different subsystems. The model configuration parameter?Reuse global block outputs  ?is selected. The state of the Unit Delay block can be optimized (that is, it does not have a global storage class or is logged signal).

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for model containing Stateflow C action language chart containing a "->" dereference of a custom code function call
Description: Simulink might display an assertion error when you generate code for a model containing a Stateflow chart that meets all of the following conditions: The Stateflow chart uses C as the action language The chart contains a statement in which a custom code function is directly dereferenced using the arrow operator "->"?  The assertion error contains the following text and the line number may vary based on release:   failed assertion in dom_core/optimization/shrinkTypes/ShrinkTypesImpl.cpp at line 536:cg_type_is_pointer(nodePtrType) For an example, see the file issue_model_1451793.slx in the attached issue_example_1451793.zip. This model contains a Stateflow chart with a statement that dereferences a custom code function call with the arrow operator: my_custom_code_fcn()->my_data = 200;
Workaround: To workaround this issue first assign the function call to a temporary variable and dereference the temporary variable. For an example, see the file issue_workaround_1451793.slx. In this model, the statement is rewritten:temp = my_custom_code_fcn();temp->my_data = 200;

Summary: signalbuilder function fails for Signal Builder blocks created prior to R2006a and successive copies of?these blocks
Description: The signalbuilder  function may fail with the message that the input block is not a Signal Builder block, even if the block is a valid Signal Builder block. This issue occurs for Signal Builder blocks created prior to Simulink version R2006a and successive copies of these blocks.
Workaround: In the Simulink Editor, select the Signal Builder block.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter this command:?set_param(gcb, 'MaskType', 'Sigbuilder block');Save the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may contain an incorrect typecast for a nonreusable subsystem with Casting Modes parameter set to Standards Compliant
Description: The generated code may contain an incorrect typecast for a model that meets these conditions: In the?Subsystem block parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation  tab, the Function packaging  parameter is set to Nonreusable function . In the incorrect generated code, the function return value is cast to a Boolean  which is smaller than the type of the function return value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: AUTOSAR model with latched root inport feeding Switch block incorrectly generates multiple Rte_Read API calls
Description: AUTOSAR code generation incorrectly generates multiple Rte_Read  API calls if all  of the following conditions are true: In the model, a root inport directly feeds a subsystem. The root inport is configured for AUTOSAR explicit read access. In the subsystem, the inport block corresponding to the root inport is latched. This includes Simulink server function subsystems for which all inports are latched. The latched subsystem inport directly feeds a Switch block data input.  Under these conditions, the generated C code contains multiple Rte_Read  API calls. This is incorrect because explicit read access APIs should not be called more than once in an AUTOSAR runnable task.
Workaround: To work around this bug, change the AUTOSAR data access mode for the root inport from explicit read to implicit read.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect code generation for nested handle objects with different lifetimes
Description: Code generation may generate incorrect code for MATLAB code with all of these conditions: An outer object that contains another handle object as its property. The inner object is assigned inside the constructor of the outer object. The lifetime of the inner object is greater than the lifetime of the outer object (there is a function whose caller(s) can access the inner object but not the outer object).  For example, the following MATLAB program meets these conditions and generates incorrect code: function y = foo(n)    o = sub(n);        y = o.p;endfunction o = sub(n)    coder.inline('never')    obj = MyOuter(n);    o = obj.p;end? classdef MyOuter < handle    properties        p;    end    methods        function o = MyOuter(n)            o.p = MyInner(n);        end    end?? end  In this program, the inner object can be accessed in function foo  because it is returned from function sub , but the lifetime of the outer object is limited only to the function sub .

Summary: Importing is slow when using readtable on a text file with many variables
Description: When using readtable  to import data from a text file, the import might be slow.
Workaround: Provide input arguments to specify the Format, ?Delimiter, ?HeaderLines, and ReadVariableNames?parameters.For example:?T = readtable(filename,'Format','%f%s%f','Delimiter',',','HeaderLines',0,'ReadVariableNames',true);?If using readtable on many files with similar formats or reading the same file multiple times, use the detectImportOptions function and re-use the detected options.?For example: ?opts = detectImportOptions(filename);T = readtable(filename,opts);?

Summary: Legend text format incorrect when specifying name-value pair arguments and multiple output arguments
Description: The legend  function ignores name-value pair arguments related to text formatting when you call legend  with multiple output arguments. For example, this code tries to set the legend's Interpreter  property to 'off' . The name-value pair argument is ignored, so the text is interpreted using the default interpreter (which is TeX): % Display figure figure plot(1:10) hold on plot(2:2:20)   % Add legend [Legend, Icons, Plots, Text] = legend({'test_01' 'test_02'},'Interpreter','none');  Other properties such as 'TextColor'  and 'FontSize'  exhibit similar formatting issues.?
Workaround: Set the correct property values after calling legend. For example, this code sets the Interpter for all Text objects to 'none':% Identify each text object and update the Interpreter propertyfor i = 1:length(Icons)? ?if strcmpi(Icons(i).Type,'text')? ? ??set(Icons(i),'Interpreter','none')? ?endend

Summary: Incorrect output when using newer GPUs with convolutional neural network functions such as trainNetwork, classify and activations
Description: Neural Network Toolbox uses the cuDNN Deep Learning library to train and execute convolutional neural networks on the GPU. The version of this library used by MATLAB does not work correctly for GPUs with a compute capability of 6.0 or above, including the latest Pascal architecture cards. If you build any neural networks using trainNetwork  with one of these cards, the network is invalid. When running trainNetwork  with verbose output, you see that accuracy is poor and the algorithm fails to converge.  classify , predict  and activations  methods of SeriesNetwork  give incorrect answers with convolutional neural networks.  An existing valid network built using an older card also gives incorrect answers on a newer GPU. To see if your GPU is affected, in MATLAB, enter:   gpuDevice  Examine the field ComputeCapability . If this value is 6.0  or higher, your card will not work with convolutional neural networks.
Workaround: Install the attached patch to fix this bug.NOTE: Users of Pascal cards in MATLAB up to R2016b experience a delay on the first use of the GPU in MATLAB. It takes time for the CUDA driver to compile the GPU libraries into executable code compatible with the GPU. To avoid this delay each time you run MATLAB, increase the CUDA cache size to cache the code instead of compiling again, by setting the following environment variable:CUDA_CACHE_MAXSIZE 536870912This sets the CUDA cache size to 512MB which is the minimum we recommend (the default is 32MB). Summary: Incorrect cast of absolute values for fixed-point types in C action language charts
Description: Fixed-point values will be cast to the size of the target int , potentially truncating the most significant bits, when all of the following conditions are true:  The chart uses C action language? The chart contains a call to the abs () function The argument of abs () is of a fixed-point type longer than int  size  For example, the value of an abs () argument with 32-bit fixed-point type will be truncated when a 16-bit int ?is specified in Configuration Parameters > Hardware Implementation > Number of bits .

Summary: Dimension validation does not work as intended for Data Store Memory block with initial value set by an array of structures
Description: When you use an array of structures to configure the initial value of a Data Store Memory block with a compatible bus object data type, an error is not reported when the dimension does not match. The unreported mismatch can result in non-deterministic memory issues during simulation. Code generation is not affected.?

Summary: Removing signal highlighting is disabled in libraries
Description: When you select a signal in a library and select?Highlight Signal to Source  or Highlight Signal to Destination , the diagram appearance and interactions change. The?Remove Highlighting ?menu item and shortcut are disabled, providing no?way to return to normal diagram editing.
Workaround: Close and reopen the library.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when pressing keyboard keys or clicking inside a figure window
Description: On Microsoft Windows systems, the first keyboard press or mouse click inside a figure window might cause MATLAB to crash.? The generated stack trace contains a line similar to: [14] 0x000000006235812b jkey_to_ukey at b:\matlab\src\uij\awtsupport.cpp

Summary: Coverage analysis of MATLAB Function block containing relational operation with complex fixed-point value causes fatal assertion
Description: A fatal assertion might occur when analyzing coverage for a model with a MATLAB Function block containing a relational operation within a condition where one or more of the operands has a complex, fixed-point datatype. ?For example, consider a MATLAB Function block containing the following if  statement comparing an input variable to a complex, fixed-point value of 1i . ?if input == fi(1i)  ?  When simulated with coverage enabled, an error message similar to the following might be shown:?failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\type\fixpttype.cpp at line 334:   error: FixptType::createFxpDtpProp: failed with unknown constructor or wrong number of bits.?

Summary: Range analysis using Fixed-Point Tool might not produce results on Windows? due to case sensitivity in working directory name
Description: Deriving ranges using the Fixed-Point?Tool might not produce?results?if the current working directory name shown in the MATLAB desktop?has any letter with a different case from the actual operating system path name. To see the actual operating system path name, use the File Explorer.?Certain Windows? file systems are not case sensitive and allow different case paths to resolve to the same folder.   An example of a current working directory in the MATLAB?desktop that might cause range analysis to fail is d:\WorK ,  while the actual operating system path?name for?the same directory is D:\work . In this case, the range analysis process finishes without showing any errors, but the derived range results are empty.
Workaround: To get results from the analysis, change the current working directory to match the actual operating system path, or run the attached script?before?running analysis.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible wrong answer for chol?in simulation and generated code when invoked with?two output arguments
Description: Code generated from MATLAB code that calls the chol  function with?two output arguments, for example   [y,p] = chol(x); might not match MATLAB when x  contains a diagonal element?that is complex with a nonzero imaginary part.  In MATLAB, p ?is equal to the column containing the complex diagonal element and y ?is? truncated to contain the Cholesky decomposition for the columns and rows smaller than this value of p .??In simulation, MEX, and generated standalone code, the complex diagonal element?is ignored and the result?is not truncated. This issue occurs for: MEX Simulation code Standalone code generated ?in MATLAB R2016a? with?an external LAPACK library specified by the CustomLAPACKCallback? parameter.  when both of the following conditions are true: x  contains a complex diagonal element whose imaginary part is nonzero. x ?has a ?fixed size with 64 or more elements or x ?has a?variable size.

Summary: International characters not accepted for parameter values?in Configuration Parameters dialog box
Description: If you enter?international characters for a parameter value on the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the characters?might be ignored and replaced with question marks ('?'). ?For example, if you include non-English characters as part of a file name, the software replaces those characters with question marks. ?

Summary: Intensive streaming to the Simulation Data Inspector or Dashboard blocks can lead to slow simulation due to excessive memory consumption.
Description: Due to memory leaks, intensive use of signal streaming to Simulation Data Inspector  or Dashboard  blocks can lead to excessive memory consumption over time, resulting in slow performance and possible crashes.
Workaround: If you observe slow performance or excessive memory usage while streaming signals with the Simulation Data Inspector or Dashboard blocks, restart MATLAB after your simulation completes.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code replacement?of operations with?conceptual arguments of type pointer can generate incorrect code
Description: Code replacement of operations with conceptual arguments of type pointer can generate incorrect code when all of the following conditions are true: Code generation uses a custom code replacement library that replaces an operation with conceptual arguments of type pointer.  The code replacement entry specifies an implementation argument with a base type that is not equal to the base type of the data passed to the replacement function.   For example, a code replacement entry matches the following memcpy  operation: int16_T data_in[n];int16_T data_out[n];memcpy(&data_out[0], &data_in[0], n*sizeof(int16_T));  The implementation representation in the code replacement entry has the following prototype: void* my_custom_memcpy(uint8_T*, uint8_T*, size_t);  In this case, the code generator generates the following incorrect code: uint8_T tmp_in = (uint8_T) data_in[0];uint8_T tmp_out = (uint8_T) data_out[0];my_custom_memcpy(&tmp_out, &tmp_in,  n*sizeof(int16_T));

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For code generation, the conv2 function may not propagate NaN values from the input to the output

For this to occur, all three of the following conditions must be true: The code is generated for simulation in a MATLAB Function block in Simulink or?MEX-file.?  The number of rows of the first input to conv2  exceeds 128.?  The BLAS AXPY function? that is called by conv2  does a quick return when its coefficient input is zero.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inport block inside a function-call subsystem does not latch feedback signal when connected to a?conditionally executed?input branch
Description: When an Inport block?inside a function-call subsystem selects the Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs  parameter, it should copy the value of the corresponding input to the subsystem before executing any other blocks inside this subsystem. This prevents the input value from changing during the execution of the subsystem. However, when the Inport bock is connected to the input of a block that supports conditional input branch execution, such as a Switch block, the Inport block fails to latch the subsystem input value. The example model mG1430178.slx in the attached ZIP-file shows the problem. When simulating the model or executing the generated code of the model, yout.signals(1).values is different from yout.signals(2).values. The logged values of Out2 are correct, while the values of Out1 are wrong.  The script checkForG1430178.m also in the attached ZIP-file detects the problem. In the MATLAB Command Window, enter ????? unlatchedInport = checkForG1430178('mG1430178')   The script lists the Inport blocks that fail to latch. If the return value for unlatchedInport is: Not empty, the model has latch problems. For the example model mG1430178, the return value is??? 1ˇÁ2 cell array??? 'mG1430178/Inport has latch problem/Function-Call Subsystem/feedback' [1]  Empty, the model is not affected by this problem.??? 0ˇÁ2 empty cell array
Workaround: Consider one of the following workarounds:Clear the Configuration Parameters> Optimization > Advanced parameters > Conditional input branch execution checkbox, or in the MATLAB Command Window, enter????? set_param(modelname, 'ConditionallyExecuteInputs', 'off')For the Inport block, clear the 'Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs' check box.Insert a Function-Call Feedback Latch block before the corresponding subsystem input port.

Summary: Error 'Unable to locate report generator binary file' when running polyspace-report-generator?
Description: When you use the polyspace-report-generator  command from the Windows command line, you get the following error message: Fatal error: Unable to locate report generator binary file:  C:\Users\userloginname\.\..\..\bin\win32\ps_rptgen.exe  Even if the path to MATLAB is added to the %PATH%  environment variable, the command does not detect the path to the polyspace-report-generator  executable.
Workaround: When running the command, provide the complete path to the executable:"C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R1015b\polyspace\bin\polyspace-report-generator" ...Alternatively, provide the relative path from the current location:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2015b\> polyspace\bin\polyspace-report-generator ...

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for AUTOSAR model writes uninitialized value to root outport when expression folding is disabled
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model generates an?Rte_Write ?function call for an uninitialized root outport if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to?ExplicitSend . The root outport value is driven by enabled subsystem output. The model is configured to disable the parameter?Configuration Parameters >?Eliminate superfluous local variables (expression folding) .  Under these conditions, the generated code code contains incorrect unconditional?Rte ?function calls to write conditionally initialized values to the root outport.

Summary: Generating C/C++ code for a subsystem with a bus input can result in an error
Description: Generating C/C++ code for a subsystem (right-click on a subsystem -> C/C++ code -> Build This Subsystem) with a bus input can produce an error similar to this: ### Build procedure for model: 'MYMODEL' aborted due to an error.? Selected signal 'XXX' in the Bus Selector block 'MYMODEL/YYY' cannot be found in the input bus signal.  ?
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.Mac Systems: If you are using Safari on a Mac, disable automatic unzipping of the downloaded file by unchecking Open Safe Files After Downloading in Preferences > General.   For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it. To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Mac Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Mac):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>On a Mac, include the .app extension in the folder name.         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Some optimization parameters?missing from Configuration Parameters dialog box?when Simulink Coder is not licensed
Description: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, Default parameter behavior  (DefaultParameterBehavior ), Inline invariant signals  (  InlineInvariantSignals )  , and the Configure? button are not available unless Simulink Coder is licensed and installed. The parameters and button should also be available when either HDL Coder or PLC Coder is licensed and installed.
Workaround: Use the set_param and get_param commands to view and modify the parameter values. ? A Simulink.Parameter object can be used to configure tunable parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible incorrect answer for division of an?int64 by a double?
Description: Suppose that??n  is an int64  and x  is a double. If  x is  -0, code generated for the   division n/x might return a different result than the result returned by MATLAB?.  In this case, MATLAB returns:   intmin('int64'),   ?if the numerator is positive   intmax('int64'),   ?if the numerator is negative  The generated code returns:  intmax('int64'),  ? if the numerator is positive? intmin('int64'),   ?if the numerator is negative   The   same issue occurs for x/n,  where x  is a double and  n is an  int64.
Workaround: Rewrite the? the division as multiplication by the reciprocal of x. For example:  n*(1/x)Your hardware must ?support nonfinite values, such as? Inf and -Inf to utilize this workaround.

Summary: Reading an .XLS file using xlsread in basic mode causes MATLAB? to crash
Description: Reading a corrupt .XLS  Excel? file, using the xlsread  function in basic  mode, causes MATLAB  to crash. For example: xlsread('my_corrupt_file.xls', '', '', 'basic'); Note:  basic  mode is the default for computers without Excel for Windows.
Workaround: Open the file and re-save it using Excel. The xlsread function should then be able to read the file.

Description: In the Simulink Real-Time FPGA I/O workflow, if the subsystem targeting the FPGA has any space or newline in it's name, the generated model fails to update with the error message: FPGA bit stream file, "FILENAME", is missing. This happens with the following boards: IO321, IO321-05, IO331, IO331-06.

Summary: MATLAB System Error when invoking multiple analyses in Simulink Design Verifier simultaneously
Description: If you invoke multiple analyses in Simulink Design Verfier, MATLAB terminates with a "MATLAB System Error " message. In earlier releases, Simlunk Design Verifier would report the error "Only one Simulink Design Verifier analysis can be run at the same time " in such a scenario.

Summary: Crash when simulating a model saved with the same name as a MATLAB built-in function
Description: MATLAB might crash if you run a Simulink model with the same name as a MATLAB built-in function (for example, "disp.slx"). ? ?
Workaround: Save the model using a different name. You can identify a MATLAB built-in function using the following command:>>? exist(modelNameString, 'builtin') == 5?where modelNameString is the intended model name, without the file extension.

Summary: Some date property values might be garbled when retrieved from a COM object
Description: When a VT_DATE  property value is retrieved from a COM object, the value might display incorrectly. A date value stored in a VT_VARIANT  property is not affected by this problem and is retrieved correctly.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder. You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog box does not open for model configured with uninstalled hardware support package
Description: Opening the Configuration Parameters dialog box for a model that is configured to use an uninstalled hardware support package fails with the following error: An unexpected erroroccurred while creating the Configuration Parameters Dialog.??Thismight be caused by functions in your MATLAB path that shadow built-in MATLABfunctions.?   ?   ????Indexexceeds matrix dimensions.?   ?   ????file:C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2016b_Nucleo\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ConfigSetDialogController\getConfigSetDialogSchema.p?   ?   ????name:getConfigSetDialogSchema?   ???line: 0    ?
Workaround: Installthe hardware support package required by the model configuration.?To determine the configured package, execute:get_param(model, 'HardwareBoard') ?Alternatively,to change the model configuration so that no hardware support package isrequired, execute:set_param(model, 'HardwareBoard', 'None')

Summary: Cursor?can disappear after pinch-zooming in the editor?on macOS 10.11 or later
Description: On a Mac running macOS 10.11 (El Capitan) or later,?using the track pad?pinch-to-zoom gesture with the Simulink editor causes the?cursor to disappear. The cursor reappears when you move it?over a window?outside of?Simulink.
Workaround: Pressing Esc?might?cause the cursor to reappear.

Summary: Simulink Editor appears not to respond to input when Save As dialog box is obscured
Description: When you close a dirty model, the Save As dialog box can be placed behind other windows. The Simulink Editor does not accept input when the dialog box is open. Because you cannot see the dialog box, it appears that Simulink is not responding.  On Windows, this issue applies to R2016a. On Linux, it applies to R2015b and earlier.
Workaround: If it appears that the Simulink Editor is not responding, minimize that window to see if a Save As dialog box is open. Respond to the dialog box to continue editing.

Summary: Generated code for Rate Limiter block may be dead if Rising slew rate parameter is set to Inf
Description: The code generator may generate dead code for a Rate Limiter block with the Rising slew rate  parameter set to Inf . Dead code is a block of code that cannot be reached via any execution path. The dead code is similar to the following code: If x > Inf {x=x+Inf}  The above code is dead because x  cannot be greater than an infinite number. Note that certification standards require that there is no dead code. Also, dead code takes up a certain amount of ROM and RAM space during execution time. ?
Workaround: Instead of using a Rate Limiter block, use Bias, Max, and Unit Delay blocks. Set them up as show in the attached image. On the Bias block parameters dialog box, set the Bias parameter to the Falling Slew Rate (R) multiplied by the Sample Time (Ts).

Summary: Block names are lost in Model Advisor generated Word or PDF format report
Description: When Model Advisor generates a report in Word or PDF format,?the path to Simulink blocks can be?lost. Instead of showing?the path of the violated blocks, Model Advisor generates?some internal flags.?

Summary: Code generation error for 2-D AUTOSAR.Parameter data object used in referenced model
Description: In an AUTOSAR model, if a referenced model uses an AUTOSAR.Parameter  data object configured for two dimensions, code generation might fail with an error similar to the following: Value for AUTOSAR.Parameter object "Table" is not scalar or one-dimensional. Specify a one-dimensional value for the objector select the option 'Support root-level matrix I/O using one-dimensional arrays' on Model Configuration Parameters >Code Generation > AUTOSAR Code Generation Options. The workaround given in the error message does not resolve the error.
Workaround: To work around this bug, replace the referenced model that uses the 2-D AUTOSAR.Parameter data object with an atomic subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code is possible for a Variant Source block connected to a Vector Concatenate block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model containing a Variant Source block connected to a Vector Concatenate block. The incorrect generated code may be missing an assignment to the signal that leaves the Variant Source block.

Summary: Crash during simulation of model reference hierarchy linked to dictionary hierarchy
Description: When you simulate a model, MATLAB can crash if?all of these conditions are true: The model references other models (through Model blocks). The models use data dictionaries. For example,?a hierarchy of referenced dictionaries?matches the hierarchy of referenced models. In one of the referenced models, a signal line?acquires settings from a Simulink.Signal  object?(which resides in one of the dictionaries). The InitialValue  property of the object is set to a value other than the default.
Workaround: Use?one of these workarounds:Explicitly update the model diagram (for example, Ctrl+D)?before trying to simulate.Link all of the models to the same data dictionary (the top-level dictionary).

Summary: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for models with a small difference between sample times
Description: Simulink Code Inspector might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" if the model has multiple sample times that are very close in value. For example, in the attached model, the input sample time defined in the constant block is 0.10000000001 and the input sample time for the model reference is 0.1.  In this case, code inspection fails because the two sample time values are too close.
Workaround: Identify sample times that might be too close in value by using the rat function:For each sample time,  execute the following:  rat(sample_time, 10e-8)If the result of the function calls are equal, the sample time values are too close together.

Summary: Viewing protected model before generating code can throw a misleading error about incorrect password
Description: If you attempt to open the view of a protected model before generating code, the protected model can throw an error indicating that an incorrect password has been used, despite your having used the correct password.
Workaround: To avoid this error, simulate or generate code directly from the protected model, without trying to view it first. Note that double-clicking on a protected model block attempts to launch a view of that model.

Summary: Double-clicking in pan, zoom, or rotate mode causes an error on a loaded figure
Description: An error occurs when you follow these steps: Create axes in R2016a or R2016b. Interact with the axes using pan, zoom, or rotate mode.? Save the figure.? Load the figure in R2015b or earlier. Enable pan, zoom, or rotate mode.? Double-click the axes.?  Double-clicking the axes should reset the view, but instead causes an error message to appear, such as: ?Reference to non-existent field 'XLim'.  Error in resetplotview>localApplyViewInfo (line 137)  ? ? ? ? ? ? set(hAxes,current_prop,viewinfo.(current_prop))  Error in resetplotview>localResetPlotView (line 85)  ? ? ? ? localApplyViewInfo(hAxes,viewinfo);  Error in resetplotview (line 48)  ? ? retval = localResetPlotView(hAxes(n),varargin{:});  Error in pan>locReturnHome (line 394)  ? ?resetplotview(ax,'ApplyStoredView');  Error in pan>locWindowButtonDownFcn (line 352)  ? ? ? ? locReturnHome(ax,hMode);  Error in hgfeval (line 62)  ? ? ? ? feval(fcn{1},varargin{:},fcn{2:end});  Error in matlab.uitools.internal.uimode/modeWindowButtonDownFcn (line 115)  hgfeval(newButtonDownFcn,hFig,evd);  Error in matlab.uitools.internal.uimode/modeControl>localModeWindowButtonDownFcn (line 157)  hThis.modeWindowButtonDownFcn(hFig,evd,hThis,newValue);  ?  Error while evaluating Figure WindowButtonDownFcn?
Workaround: Call?rmappdata(ax,'matlab_graphics_resetplotview')?where ax?is the loaded Axes object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in behavior with MATLAB when you call a method using object.(methodName)(arguments) syntax
Description: In generated code, when using dot-parentheses syntax to call a method, the object is not passed as an argument. For example, consider this MATLAB code: x.(methodName)(y,z) In MATLAB, the method designated by methodName ?receives the elements of x , y , and z  as arguments. In the generated code, the method designated by methodName  receives only y  and z  as arguments.

Summary: Simulink models with From Spreadsheet blocks stop working with Excel 2016 if there are seven or more Excel processes running
Description: On a Windows machine with MS Office Excel 2016, if the total number of Excel processes exceeds seven, the machine cannot start a new Excel process for a From Spreadsheet block that tries to access an Excel spreadsheet.
Workaround: As a workaround, install Excel version 16.0.6741.2048 or higher. For more information, see https://social.msdn.microsoft.com/Forums/sqlserver/pt-BR/3ac19fd4-491d-468d-bdd8-94c8e741a2e1/excel-2016-cannot-use-object-linking-and-embedding-after-10-instances?forum=exceldev.

Summary: Code Generation?configuration component listed in Model Explorer when Simulink Coder license is disabled
Description: If the?UseSimulinkCoderFeatures ?parameter is?not selected, the? Code Generation  pane and subpanes?are not available in the Configuration Parameters dialog box. However, if you view the configuration set in the Model Explorer, the?Code Generation? component is listed. In this case, if you right-click on the?Code Generation  component to view its properties, you see this warning: The dialog schema must be a struct.  ??

Summary: intlinprog runs out of memory while solving a large problem
Description: When  intlinprog  is solving a problem with a large number of variables, constraints, and non-zero elements in the equality and inequality constraint matrices, it can fail with an out-of-memory error. The error?occurs as intlinprog   is about to start its branch-and-bound phase.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when using Simulink Profiler with models containing delay blocks or enabled ports
Description: Delay blocks can trigger resets during simulation. However, these resets are not triggered when you profile models containing delay blocks using the Simulink Profiler. leading to inaccurate simulation results.  Similarly, for blocks that register enabled ports, the enable condition is not checked when the Simulink Profiler is in use, again leading to an inaccurate simulation results.

Summary: Code generation may fail?for?a MATLAB Function block?that contains a?repmat function?in which the second argument is a nonscalar
Description: Code generation may fail for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block that uses the repmat  function. The second argument to the repmat  function is a vector or matrix. One of the dimensions of?the vector or matrix?is a variable.
Workaround: For the repmat function, use constant values and multiple arguments instead of a vector or matrix. For example, use repmat(A,2,3) instead of repmat(A,[2,B]), where the value of B is 3.

Summary: Environment Controller block in a referenced model can cause wrong behavior when generating code
Description: If a referenced model uses model arguments, and blocks that use those arguments are feeding an Environment Controller block, then generating code for a parent model?can cause misleading errors or crashes.
Workaround: In R2016a and R2016b, for any ?Model blocks that are referencing a model with the Environment Controller block and model arguments, set the Simulation mode parameter?to Accelerator .

Summary: Crash when using a root-level Outport block with?Reusable custom storage class
Description: MATLAB might crash when you update the diagram or generate code from?the model?if you use the Model Data Editor to apply the?custom storage class Reusable  to a root-level Outport?block. To use Reusable  effectively, you must apply it to a signal object (such as Simulink.Signal ) in a workspace instead of applying it?directly to an Outport block. In R2016b, you cannot resolve a root-level Outport block to a signal object, so you cannot effectively use Reusable  with a root-level Outport block.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code may not contain the output signal of a variant subsystem when the signal has a non-auto storage class
Description: The generated code may not contain the outputsignal of a variant subsystem when the following is true:  The output signal has a non-auto storage class.  In the Variant Subsystem block parameters dialog box, the Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is selected.  In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target?file  parameter is a value other then ert.tlc .?

Summary: Creating a harness model for a subsystem with System Requirements block crashes MATLAB
Description: If you try to create a harness for a subsystem?that contains a System Requirements block, MATLAB crashes. Reproduction steps:? 1. Open a Simulink model which has a System Requirements blocks inside a subsystem. 2. Create a harness for the above subsystem (either using dialog box or the command line API).  ?

Summary: MATLAB crashes with the message sf_process_mex_Sfunction_simulink_call(...) at toolbox/stateflow/src/sf_sfun/sf_sfun.cpp
Description: In some situations, when you update or run a model containing a MATLAB Function block, MATLAB might crash with following message:  sf_process_mex_Sfunction_simulink_call(...) at toolbox/ stateflow/ src/ sf_sfun/ sf_sfun.cpp?  A common cause of this crash is simulating a MATLAB Function block model in a package folder, for example "./+My/+Package/".

Summary: Error for signals or states with name longer than 63 characters
Description: Before R2016a, you could use more than 63 characters to name a signal line or block state. The generated code preserved the full signal or state name if you applied a storage class other than Auto  through one of these methods: By using the Signal Properties dialog box (for a signal) or the block dialog box (for a state). You set Package  to None . By using the programmatic port parameter StorageClass  or block parameter StateStorageClass . You did not attach a signal object (for example, Simulink.Signal ) by using the parameters SignalObject  or StateSignalObject . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Reciprocal block returns?wrong answer?for input of -0?or -Inf for simulation and C code generation
Description: HDL Reciprocal block returns a wrong answer (Inf  or 0 ) for these?cases for simulation and C code generation:  Output?Inf  for input of -0  Output 0  for input of -Inf ?

Summary: Checking compatibility with Simulink Code Inspector?results in warning for models with Subsystem block
Description: Simulink Code Inspector displays the following warning message when you check compatibility for a model that contains a subsystem block: Warning: A value of class "double" was indexed with no subscripts specified. Currently the result of this operation is the indexed value itself, but in a future release, it will be an error.  The warning does not impact compatibility checking.

Summary: A Unit Delay block with a hybrid input sample time results in assertion during compatibility checks
Description: A Unit Delay block with a hybrid?input sample time acts as a rate transition for the input sample?times that do not match the output sample time. Simulink Code Inspector compatibility checks?report a warning when a Unit Delay block behaves as a rate transition.?However, if the input?sample time is hybrid, compatibility checking results in assertion because the check?expects?only one input sample time.
Workaround: Avoid using a Unit Delay block with a hybrid input sample time. Instead, remodel the Simulink diagram so that?a?Unit Delay block has only one discrete sample time, which is the?same for input and output.

Summary: Generated code for export-function model containing inline variants may contain extra temporary variables
Description: The generated code may contain extra temporary variables for a model that meets the following conditions: The model is set-up to export functions. The Function-call block contains a Variant Source block or a Variant Sink block. The inputs to the Function-call block have different variant conditions than the Function-call block.  Extra temporary variables and their associated data copies can reduce the performance of the generated code.
Workaround: To avoid this bug, try to set-up your model so that the inputs to the Function-call block have the same variant condition as Function-call block.

Summary: Code generation may fail for a model that contains the $A token for the?Local temporary variables or Local block output variables parameters
Description: A model may crash or code generation may fail?for a model that contains the $A  token for the Local temporary variables  or Local block output variables  parameters. These parameters are on the Code Generation>Symbols  pane. Code generation fails with an error message similar to the following: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_env.cpp line 292: nextSymbol ! =0"
Workaround: For most?models, you can avoid this bug by removing the $A token from the?naming rules for the Local temporary variables or Local block output variables?parameters.

Description: Variant subsystem with generate pre-processor conditionals will fail to build if model configuration have below settings. 1. In model configuration: BooleanAsBitFields set to true? ?? for models with Stateflow, the following are true in addition to the condition above.? 2. In model configuration 'StateAsBitSets' and 'DataBitSets' set to true. DW_min__spd;#define DW_16B__T_VARIANT_EXISTS#endif???????????????????????????????? /* Variant */} DW_reproduced_16b__T; ? ?  ? ? ? ?
Workaround: Turn off the below model configuration settings which manages bitfields in generated code.1. When the model has stateflow charts?as?variant choice blocks:? 'Use bitsets for storing state configuration'??? 'Use bitsets for storing Boolean data '??? No need to?turn off these parameters in the absence of Stateflow charts.?2.?When the model has only Simulink blocks:?'Pack Boolean data into bitfields'

Description: Multiple error messages appear when you open the Plot Browser in a figure that contains a patch object with these two settings: The CDataMapping  property is set to 'direct' . The CData  property contains values that are decimals.

Description: On MATLAB startup, MATLAB Editor may fail to launch even though it was open when MATLAB last exited. If this occurs, you might see a Java exception.  You can open MATLAB Editor again, but it does not launch with the files that were previously open.

Summary: Checks for MISRA C:2012 compliance that require the model to compile do not work for models using autosar.tlc
Description: The following checks in the Model Advisor By Task group Modeling Guidelines for MISRA C:2012  do not work when using the AUTOSAR system target file (autosar.tlc ) as the code generation target: Check for bitwise operations on signed integers Check for recursive function calls? Check for equality and inequality operations on floating-point values? Check for switch case expressions without a default case?  Model Advisor displays an error message similar to: Error occurred during model compile.     NOTE:?  This check applies to models configured for code generation.    Model 'myModel ' cannot reference 'AUTOSAR.Signal' object 'RAMBlock_2' because that model is not using an AUTOSAR target.   ?

Summary: On some Linux platforms, system might return partial results when called in a loop.?
Description: On some distributions of Linux, with certain shells, the following might return truncated results: for i = 1:100    [status,output] = system(command);end The variable output  might not contain all the text produced by the command. This issue has been identified as a Linux kernel regression which affects Linux kernels 3.12 through 4.6.  You can identify your currently running Linux kernel version by running: uname -r See: https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/commit/?id=0f40fbbcc34e093255a2b2d70b6b0fb48c3f39aa?
Workaround: Use a different sub-shell to execute the command. For example, replace the following:[status,output] = system(command);with[status,output] = system(['/bin/tcsh -c "' command '"']);This does not work with every shell.To attempt to capture all the output, call system with no command after the original command, then concatenate the output from the two commands. For example,[status,output] = system(command);[~,remainder] = system('');output = [output remainder];The preferred fix is to upgrade to a kernel version that has been patched.  This issue affects kernel version 3.12 through 4.6, and is fixed in versions 4.1.26, 4.4.12, 4.5.6, and 4.6.1.All current versions of Red Hat Enterprise Linux do not appear to be affected.Ubuntu 16.04 LTS has fixed this issue in linux-image-generic package version 4.4.0.25 or greater.Ubuntu 14.04.5 LTS has fixed this issue when using the linux-generic-lts-xenial kernel package version 4.4.0.25 or greater.Ubuntu 12.04 LTS is affected only when using 12.04.5 with the linux-generic-lts-trusty package, which updates the kernel to version 3.13.SUSE SLES 12 and SLES 12 SP1 use kernel 3.12, and do appear to be affected by this bug. There is currently no update available to fix this issue on that distribution.

Summary: Older X servers might not recognize international keyboard layouts
Description: Simulink requires the XKEYBOARD extension to detect the keyboard layout that is currently active. Some older X servers do not support this extension. If this extension is absent or disabled, then Simulink assumes a default layout. When the default layout does not match the keyboard layout, some keys might not produce the expected characters. Suppose you are using a Linux-based computer with a German keyboard. Simulink uses the default layout, which is English, and can therefore produce incorrect characters. MATLAB correctly recognizes the keyboard as German. The following X servers are currently known to have this problem: Exceed onDemand
Workaround: Please contact technical support for a workaround if you cannot?change to a supported X server.

Summary: Crash?during code generation?from model hierarchy with mismatched parameter data types
Description: When you generate code from a model, MATLAB crashes when all of these conditions are true: The model references?another model (Model block). In the referenced model: The configuration parameter Inline parameters   is cleared (before R2015b). The configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable  (including and after R2015b).  In the referenced model, a workspace variable sets the value of?a block parameter (such as the?Gain  parameter of a Gain block). The data type of the workspace variable is inconsistent with the data type of the block parameter.

Summary: When decoding JSON text, webread and jsondecode discard null values in numeric arrays. ?
Description: When numeric data have missing or unavailable values, they?are often represented in JSON with the value null.  If yo u import data from JSON text using the?webread  or jsondecode  functions, ?null  is?silently discarded from numeric arrays.

Summary: Segmentation violation or incorrect error message when building AUTOSAR model that uses Signal Invalidation block with nonscalar data and schema version below 4.0
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR model fails without displaying a useful error message when both of the following conditions exist:  The model contains a Signal Invalidation block with a data input configured for nonscalar data, such as vector or matrix data. The AUTOSAR schema version configured for the model is lower than 4.0.   Under these conditions, building the model should fail with a message noting a software limitation. Instead, the build fails with a segmentation violation (R2015b) or an incorrect error message (R2016a).
Workaround: Embedded Coder has a limitation that it cannot generate code for a Signal Invalidation block configured for nonscalar data, if the AUTOSAR schema version is lower than 4.0.To work around this limitation,?set the AUTOSAR schema version to 4.0 or higher.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector reports "Partially verified" status for commented out blocks?in model
Description: A commented out block?in a model?introduces ambiguity in the dataflow semantics of the Simulink diagram.?Blocks that feed input to or receive input?from a commented out block need to be grounded or terminated during model simulation. This creates a gap between the graphical and simulation list of blocks.? As a result, if you comment out a block, Simulink Code Inspector returns "Partially verified" for the inspection.
Workaround: Delete commented out blocks from the Simulink?model.

Summary: Generated arxml code missing VARIABLE-ACCESS node corresponding to read of AUTOSAR inter-runnable variable
Description: The arxml  file generated from an AUTOSAR model might incorrectly describe a read of an inter-runnable variable (IRV) if all  of the following conditions are true: The connected runnables are modeled using function-call inports (exported function modeling style). The subsystem inport corresponding to the read of the IRV is latched. The IRV is connected to a Switch block. Model option Conditional input branch execution  (ConditionallyExecuteInputs ) is selected.  Under these conditions, arxml  export fails to generate a VARIABLE-ACCESS node describing the read of the IRV.
Workaround: To work around this bug, clear the option Conditional input branch execution. For example:set_param(bdroot,'ConditionallyExecuteInputs','off');

Summary: Tasks that produce many suppressed warnings can cause workers to run out of memory
Description: If a task produces suppressed warnings while running, these warnings are stored in memory on the worker even though they cannot be retrieved when the task is finished. Storing suppressed warnings in memory is a bug. If many suppressed warnings are produced while a task is running, memory usage on the worker can increase significantly. If the worker runs out of memory, the task may hang or fail. Unsuppressed warnings are correctly stored in memory while the task is running so that you can retrieve them in the 'Warnings' property of the task when the task is finished.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, rewrite the code running on workers to produce as few suppressed and unsuppressed warnings as possible. A task should complete normally if the worker does not run out of memory.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code for an integer parameter with a custom storage class of Define or ImportedDefine may cause arithmetic overflow
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a Simulink.Parameter  that meets the following conditions:  The parameter has a Fixed point  or Built in  integer data type. The parameter has a Custom Storage Class of Define  or ImportedDefine . The parameter specifies a symbolic dimension and is used elsewhere in the model as part of an arithmetic expression.  In the incorrect generated code, the C compiler may treat the value of the parameter?as a different data type than is?in the Simulink model. Such a data type mismatch may cause arithmetic operations to yield incorrect results. For example, a parameter of type unsigned short  in a model may be an unsigned int  in the generated C code when it should be cast to a signed int . As a result, signed int  values may be incorrectly treated as unsigned values. When a negative int  value is wrongly interpreted as a large unsigned int  value, integer overflow occurs.
Workaround: If you have your own TLC file for the ImportedDefine or Define custom storage class, generate a data type cast to explicitly convert integers of mismatched type to the correct integer data type used in model. Perform the cast either in a?#define?statement or in the expression where the variable is referenced.

Summary: Multitasking ERT/GRT code can produce a wrong answer when ?a continuous signal drives a subsystem with a discrete base rate and a rare combination of conditions also exists
Description: Generated ERT/GRT code?can miss a major sample time hit guard for the discrete sub-rate, which?can produce a wrong answer if?all? the following conditions are true:? The Tasking Mode is set to "auto" or "Multi-Tasking" and Code Generation Interface enables "continuous time" support.? The subsystem is atomic and has a continuous signal feeding it. Inside the subsystem, there are no continuous states. ? Inside the subsystem, there is a discrete base rate and at least one discrete sub-rate. Inside the subsystem, the base rate blocks read from the incoming continuous signal. Inside the subsystem, at least one discrete sub-rate block is connected to the base rate blocks.
Workaround: Use one of these workarounds:Use single tasking.Assign any discrete rate other than the discrete base rate to the subsystem input port receiving the continuous signal.Drop a block with continuous states in the subsystem.Use an ODE1 solver.?

Summary: Simulink.Bus.CreateMATLABStruct can error when passed port handles and model uses a configuration set reference
Description: When both  these conditions are true, using Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruc t throws an error:  The function is passed a port handle or an array of port handles from bus signal ports in a model.  That model uses a configuration set reference, rather than a local configuration set.  The following error is thrown:  Error using Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>switchFlagsBeforeCompile (line 483) A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>compileModelAndGetStruct (line 423) pFlags = switchFlagsBeforeCompile(model, pFlags); Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct>createMATLABStructFromPortHandle (line 399) mStruct = compileModelAndGetStruct(model, portHandles, false, alreadyCompiled, false); Error in Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct (line 203) mStruct = createMATLABStructFromPortHandle(obj, pStruct);
Workaround: To work around this issue, attach a temporary local configuration set to the model and set it to active. After this, Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct should work as expected. Once the use of the function is complete, you can remove the local configuration set from the model.

Summary: import function does not import the specified package when run in the Live Editor
Description: When calling the import  function from a live script, MATLAB is unable to resolve imported packages or classes. This is because the imported package or class is not applied to the proper import list scope.
Workaround: Use the full package name of the function that you wish to call rather than using the import function.For example, instead of:import java.lang.Strings = String('hello');use:s = java.lang.String('hello');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Embedded Coder generates incorrect code for dimension variants with division operation
Description: Embedded Coder generates incorrect code for dimension variants with division operations, if those divide a term that contains a plus or minus operation. For example, a dimension variant with the expression (A+B)/C  is incorrectly generated as A/C+B/C .
Workaround: Avoid division operations in dimension variants.

Summary: imclose returns incorrect results along the edges
Description: The results of imclose  along a half-strel wide boundary is incorrect.  Example of the bug:  bw = imread('text.png'); strel = ones(1,15); bwc = imclose(bw, strel); % Note that foreground in bwc should not extend to the left of the first two lines of text.% This extension of the output at the boundaries is a bug. imshowpair(bw, bwc);  This behavior will impact the output only when there is a foreground pixel within a half-strel wide boundary of the input image.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, use the underlying dilate and erode operations with a padded image. bw = imread('text.png'); strel = strel(ones(1,15)); % Pad the image. padSize = ceil(size(strel.Neighborhood)/2); bwPadded = padarray(bw,padSize,'both'); % Perform the equivalent close operation using imdilate and imerode bwdPadded = imdilate(bwPadded, strel); bwePadded = imerode(bwdPadded, strel); % Un-pad the image. bwc = bwePadded(padSize(1)+1:end-padSize(1), padSize(2)+1:end-padSize(2)); figure;imshowpair(bw, bwc);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator produces incorrect code for non-inlined S-Function block in multirate model
Description: When these conditions exist, the code generator treats an S-function as a non-inlined S-function, reading time values incorrectly and producing incorrect code: Multirate model includes an S-Function block that uses absolute or elapsed time. For more information, see S-Functions for Multirate Multitasking Environments  in the Simulink Coder documentation.

Summary: Problems opening or using Simulation Data Inspector, Simulink Test blocks, and Dashboard blocks
Description: The internal data repository used by the Simulation Data Inspector, Simulink Test blocks, and Dashboard blocks can become corrupt. When the data repository is corrupt, trying to launch the Simulation Data Inspector or run the model that contains Simulink Test or Dashboard blocks causes errors or a crash. Restarting MATLAB does not resolve the problem.? The problem is specific to a single installation of MATLAB but not to a model, MAT-file, or view.?
Workaround: Before running a model or opening the Simulation Data Inspector, run this command in the MATLAB Command Window:??sdi.Repository.clearRepositoryFile();

Summary: Deployed applications with Image Acquisition Toolbox support packages error out on Linux
Description: When running a standalone?application?deployed with MATLAB Compiler that uses Image Acquisition Toolbox support packages on Linux platforms, it errors out with the following message: No Image Acquisition adaptors found. Image acquisition adaptors may be available as downloadable support packages. Open Support Package Installer to install additional adaptors.
Workaround: 1. Compile the application, making sure to include the support package when using deploytool, or using the -a flag with mcc.?2. Set the LD_LIBRARY_PATH as described in the following documentation page:https://www.mathworks.com/help/compiler_sdk/java/mcr-path-settings-for-run-time-deployment.html?3. Set the value of the environment variable MCR_CACHE_ROOT.This is the path where the CTF (Component Technology File) will get extracted. For instance, on a tcsh shell, use: setenv MCR_CACHE_ROOT /home/usr/tmp?4. Append this path to the LD_LIBRARY_PATH: MCR_CACHE_ROOT/<path to bin/glnxa4>For instance, this path would be: /home/usr/tmp/.mcrCache9.0/myImaqApp/bin/glnxa64 where myImaqApp is the name of the deployed app.?5. Then run the app using ./myImaqApp.Do not run the generated?script that sets the LD_LIBRARY_PATH and then runs the app, because that will overwrite what was done in step 4.

Summary: On OS X, saving a model that prompts to update history hangs MATLAB
Description: On OS X, saving a model that you set up to prompt to update model history hangs MATLAB. When the Add Comment to Model History dialog box appears, you cannot enter text in the dialog box or at the MATLAB command prompt. This issue occurs whether you set the mo del parameter interactively or programmatically, that is: In the model properties, set Prompt to update model history  to When saving model . Set the UpdateHistory  parameter on the model to 'UpdateHistoryWhenSave' .

Summary: Compiled applications might not automatically start a parallel pool when run
Description: A parallel pool might fail to start, with an undefined function  error, when a compiled application includes a parfor  loop, spmd  block, or some other parallel language element that should automatically open a pool.
Workaround: You can use either of the two workarounds below to prevent this issue. Workaround 1: applied before packaging Use the gcp('nocreate') function in your compiled MATLAB code.Workaround 2: applied before packaging ?Add the following pragma code?%#function gcp at the top of your compiled MATLAB code to notify MATLAB Compiler that Parallel Computing Toolbox functionality is required.

Summary: Generated code may contain arrays?with fixed upper bounds instead of dimension variants for exported subsystem functions
Description: The generated code for a subsystem may contain arrays with fixed upper bounds instead of dimension variants when you right-click the subsystem and select C/C++ Code>Export Functions .
Workaround: Convert the subsystem to a referenced model and then?generate code for the top model.?The code generator exports functions for the referenced model.

Summary: Incorrect Relational Boundary coverage in MATLAB code following conditions with enum or Boolean operands
Description: This bug affects Relational Boundary coverage of MATLAB code (such as in MATLAB Function blocks or Stateflow Charts using MATLAB Action Language) where the code contains a condition comparing Boolean or enum operands. Relational Boundary coverage is intentionally not instrumented for relational operations where both operands are Boolean or at least one operand is an enum. However, if the MATLAB code being analyzed contains such an uninstrumented condition, then the Relational Boundary coverage objectives for all subsequent conditions in the code show incorrect results. Specifically, the coverage results are shifted such that each Relational Boundary coverage objective shows the results for the previous objective. The objective associated with the relational operation that immediately follows the uninstrumented enum/Boolean comparison shows that none of its outcomes were satisfied or executed. However, the objectives associated with the relational operations that precede the uninstrumented enum/Boolean comparison are not affected by this bug and still show correct coverage results. In some cases, this behavior may be accompanied by an assertion failure that is reported at the MATLAB command line and in the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer. In such cases, one of the following two messages may be shown:  failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\src\slvnv\utils\object.cpp at line 2842: id<pool_capacity  failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\slvnv\simcoverage\src\slvnv\utils\object.cpp at line 2844: object[id]->isa==isa

Summary: Aggressive compiler optimizations may invalidate data protection algorithm for a Rate Transition block operating in data integrity mode
Description: Some advanced compilers, such as GCC 4.9 -03 on x86 platforms, use aggressive optimization options that can reorder instructions for critical section algorithms produced by a Rate Transition block. For reordering to?occur, in the?Block Parameters dialog box, the Rate Transition block must have the following settings:?  The Ensure data integrity during data transfer  parameter is selected. The Ensure deterministic data transfer  parameter is cleared.?  Instruction reordering of the critical section algorithm is rare because compiler instruction scheduling depends on many factors such as compiler type, processor architecture, and model data alignment.  You can run the attached MATLAB script to check whether a model contains any Rate Transition blocks that could potentially be affected by this bug because they have the above settings. MATLAB R2013a or later is required to run this script.
Workaround: Particularly if you are deploying?rate transition code in safety-critical systems, use one of the following workarounds:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Code Generation pane, turn off the compiler optimization flag when compiling the generated ERT/GRT code. For example, for a GCC compiler, set the flag to -O0.In the Rate Transition Block Parameters dialog box, change the parameter settings as follows:- Select the Ensure data integrity during data transfer parameter.- Select the Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay) parameter.??

Summary: Custom menus do not appear in some Simulink Editor windows
Description: Custom menu items installed to either Simulink:MenuBar  or Stateflow:MenuBar  do not appear in some models when either  of these situations occur: You open a model with a Simulink Editor is active and the custom item was installed for Stateflow:MenuBar.   You opn a model with a Stateflow Editor is active and the custom item was installed for Simulink:MenuBar .
Workaround: To avoid this issue:1. In your customization file, install the menu item for both Simulink:MenuBar and Stateflow:MenuBar. cm.addCustomMenuFcn( 'Simulink:MenuBar', @generator ); cm.addCustomMenuFcn( 'Stateflow:MenuBar', @generator ); 2. In your custom menu item generator, modify the state of the ToolSchema to be hidden when it should be. For example, if you want the custom item to appear only when a Simulink Editor is active, use the following code to hide it when a Stateflow Editor is active. function schema = myMenuItem( cbinfo )     schema = sl_action_schema;     ...     if ~isa( cbinfo.domain, 'SLM3I.SLDomain' )         schema.state = 'Hidden';     end endTo test for Stateflow, use:if isa( cbinfo.domain, 'StateflowDI.SFDomain' ) ... end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation and code generation mismatch due to execution order change
Description: When a sink block, such as Scope, Display, or Outport block, is added or removed from a block diagram, the block sorted execution order may change. If Data Store Read and Data Store Write blocks mapped to the same Data Store Memory are affected by such change, it may lead to different answers when the relative execution order changes. In particular, certain sink blocks such as Scope and Display will execute in normal simulation but will be left out during code generation; thus the presence of such blocks can lead to a mismatch in the results between normal simulation and generated code.

Summary: Text generated by ylabel can overlap Y-axis tick labels of a plotmatrix graph.
Description: When you label a y-axis with the ylabel  function, it can overprint tick labels produced by plotmatrix . For example: %%% BEGIN CODE %%%M = repmat(100 * magic(4), 10, 1);plotmatrix(M);hx = xlabel('simple x-label');hy = ylabel('simple y-label');%%% END CODE %%%
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can manually adjust the position of the Y-label. To do this programmatically:%%% BEGIN CODE %%%ylpos = get(hy, 'position');set(hy, 'position', [ylpos(1) - 0.04, ylpos(2 : 3)]);%%% END CODE %%%To adjust the label interactively:Enter plot edit mode (Tools -> Edit plot)Select the Y-label by clicking itDrag the Y-label with the mouse to the left until it no longer overlaps the tick labels..
